description: The frontend forecast engine (roadmap-forecast.ts) never consumed member tasks — it only ran the committed-window + effort basis — so any objective with dated member tasks silently disagreed with the CLI/snapshot engine (roadmap-model.ts), which treats the task span as the schedule of record. Visible failure: a ROLLUP objective that BOTH depends_on its sub-objectives AND shares their member tasks — the timeline pushed forecast_start to the dependency finish then stacked the rollup's own effort on top, but that own work IS the shared tasks it already counted via the dependency, inflating the bar into a phantom slip while the CLI read on track. buildForecasts now mirrors roadmap-model.ts three-branch order exactly: (1) dated member tasks give a span (earliest start to latest due) clamped to dep finishes, effort NOT re-added; (2) else committed start gives window + effort; (3) else deps give milestone + effort. Each Forecast carries a basis of tasks|window|milestone|none; ObjectiveDetailPanel uses it to attribute a slip and caption the footer. Adds a cross-engine parity test driving both engines through real scratch vaults.
summary: Timeline forecast honours the task-date basis (kills phantom rollup slip)

description: Once a project had a git origin, Settings then Cloud sync showed nothing between GitHub sign-in and Linked repos — OriginSetup self-hid the moment an origin existed, leaving the connected repo invisible and unmanageable. OriginSetup now renders a connected-origin card when an origin is set: View (repo name linking to GitHub + full remote URL), Change (re-point origin at a different reachable repo, preview-gated; reverts sync to in-tree so no background pull races the new remote's history), and Disconnect (confirm-gated detach that removes origin + reverts to in-tree; refuses with 409 merge_in_progress while a merge is live). Backend: POST /api/brain/origin/update and /detach, detachProjectOrigin() + disableFullRepo(). Frontend: useUpdateOrigin/useDetachOrigin hooks + card + 8 i18n keys. Security review PASS (git via execFileSync argv, canonicalRemote rebuild, fixed api.github.com host, no token leak). +5 lib/route tests.
summary: Manage the connected origin — view / change / disconnect in Settings

description: A long-lived desktop dashboard server holds its route table and frontend bundle in memory, so after `dreamcontext upgrade` / `npm i -g` it kept serving the OLD build (stale UI, missing sections, old routes) until manually quit + relaunched; macOS had no auto-heal (the drift self-exit is excluded for the desktop app and the ensure-dashboard heal was Windows-only). Now: server-side startUpgradeReadyWatch (desktop-gated) flags a strictly-newer on-disk version (compareVersions > 0, so a downgrade never force-relaunches), surfaced on /api/health as upgradeReady; an UpgradeRelaunchBanner auto-relaunches the whole app (close windows then app quits then detached open reopens on the fresh CLI), checking the relaunch ok before quitting so a failed relaunch never dead-ends, with an 8s stuck-spinner safety net and a 20s countdown + Later escape; launcher dedups in-flight /launcher/relaunch; version-check reads the CLI version fresh from disk so the update badge self-clears. Ships via npm (no Tauri rebuild).
summary: Desktop app auto-relaunches after an in-place upgrade (macOS self-heal)

description: The desktop app disables the OS credential helper and authenticates git only via its stored global OAuth token, while three surfaces read three uncoordinated signals — the sidebar read the real git result, /brain/auth/status reported "connected" on token PRESENCE alone, and the CLI used a separate keychain credential. A stale app token made the on-open pull throw auth ("GitHub sign-in expired") while Settings still showed a valid session and the CLI synced fine. Fix makes the ACTUAL git auth result the single source of truth: new global needsReconnect flag written off the real git outcome (set on a classified auth-reject, cleared on any authenticated result; never marks-invalid with no token), surfaced on /brain/auth/status so the Settings chip and sidebar can't disagree; classifySyncError splits "no token" from "expired/invalid"; Settings renders an invalid chip + in-place Reconnect; a sync refreshes auth-status in real time. +unit/route tests.
summary: Stop false 'GitHub sign-in expired' when the session is actually valid

description: A dreamcontext brain can now govern bare code repositories via a two-layer model: shared {name, gitRemoteUrl} in .config.json (team-synced when cloud-sync is on); machine-local url→path registry (~/.dreamcontext/linked-repos.json, never synced). 'Link' is a pointer, not a pipe — records existence + local location, never syncs code. New 'dreamcontext link' CLI (add/list/remove/clone); GET+POST /api/linked-repos server routes; dashboard Linked Repos panel in Settings (Add / Clone missing / Remove). Trust-gated clone: user confirms the repo URL before any git operation. Security ordering: S1 (RCE/arg-injection guard BEFORE any git call — parseRepoSlug + canonicalRemote rebuild), S2 (path-traversal containment — sanitizeRepoName + resolve + startsWith guard), S3 (governed-repo origin-match checks in linkRepo). git.ts clone() globally hardened with -c protocol.ext.allow=never + -- terminator. lib: src/lib/linked-repos.ts; CLI: src/cli/commands/link.ts; routes: src/server/routes/linked-repos.ts; dashboard: LinkedRepos.tsx + useLinkedRepos hook; +4 unit/integration tests. Uncommitted (session 94eda2ea).
summary: Linked repos: machine-local registry + link CLI + trust-gated clone + dashboard panel

description: The resting sync label decayed to 'Project synced' once the 4s transient feedback cleared — even while a persistent failure banner (expired GitHub sign-in, scrub block, deferred merge, detached HEAD) was still on screen. The footer label now mirrors the last known sync state in warn color and only falls back to 'Project synced' when the last sync genuinely succeeded; a new blockedNote signal covers the two panel-less blocked outcomes. The label is now a role=status aria-live region.

summary: ⌘⇧D spawns a terminal split (agent stays ⌘D)

description: New checkTaskFeatureLinks audit surfaces every kind of task↔feature link drift (ghost/ambiguous related_feature, conflicting/foreign claims, missing back-refs or memberships, non-canonical slugs). dreamcontext doctor --heal-links applies the deterministic fixes in one shot — adopt back-refs, drop ghost/foreign related_tasks entries, canonicalize slugs — before running the checks.
summary: doctor --heal-links reconciles task↔feature link drift

description: Whole-project (full-repo) sync pushes to the project's own origin, but after the separate-mode removal a project with no origin dead-ended at a bare 400 no_origin. Settings → Cloud sync now offers, when signed in and the project has no origin: Create new (a fresh private-by-default GitHub repo wired as origin) or Connect existing (an existing repo URL). It then enables full-repo, lays the gitignore-first excludes, and runs the first auto-sync to bootstrap+push. Not the old dedicated brain repo — rooted at the project root, no discovery marker. lib: src/lib/git-sync/origin-setup.ts; routes: POST /api/brain/origin/{create,preview,attach}; dashboard: OriginSetup panel + hooks + i18n; +22 unit/route-guard tests.
summary: Create/attach a GitHub origin from the Cloud sync panel

description: Make task→feature assignment real and keep both sides in lockstep. New feature-links.ts engine: canonical (folder-qualified) feature resolution, bidirectional apply (task.related_feature ↔ feature.related_tasks), task rename/delete healing, and audit/reconcile — all reads/writes guarded against path traversal. CLI: tasks create --feature, new tasks feature <task> [ref|clear] (mirrors tasks objectives), and create-time nudges when a link is unset. features set/create related_tasks now validate slugs and write through to each task's related_feature (ghost slugs refused). Server PATCH /api/tasks/:slug validates objectives + related_feature (400 on unknown/ambiguous) and write-throughs the feature side. graph.ts draws task→feature edges by slug (was id-only, so real links drew none); snapshot renders each task's Feature inline. Dashboard feature picker emits folder-qualified slugs; invalidates knowledge query on related_feature change. 11 files, 1266 insertions. +19-test feature-links engine suite, golden PATCH-route coverage, and cli-commands integration coverage for create/set validation + write-through. Backfilled this brain's links: 35 tasks now carry related_feature (was 1). Held back from this commit (file-level tangled with concurrent brain-sync refactor): doctor --heal-links wiring in doctor.ts and skill/agent docs (to be committed once that work settled).
summary: Validated, bidirectional, self-healing task↔feature links

description: The timeline forecast slid an objective's full committed-window width forward on every dependency push (forecast_end = forecast_start + windowWidth), so a terminal objective slipped past its target even when its dependencies were on time and no effort was set. The frontend (slide) and server (clamp) also silently disagreed, and the effort field was captured but never touched the forecast. Unified both engines on an effort-aware, envelope-clamped finish-to-start model: forecast_start = max(committed start, max dep forecast_end); workEnd = forecast_start + effort×7; forecast_end = max(committed end, workEnd) — bar never shrinks below the window; slipping = target set AND workEnd > target; slipDays = workEnd − target (negative = buffer/runway). An on-time dependency now consumes an objective's slack, not its deadline (no effort + deps on time = on track); effort drives a real, sized slip when the work can't fit before the target. The server also forecasts committed-window objectives that have no dated tasks (were unforecastable), so the CLI/snapshot match the timeline. Objectives with dated tasks keep the task-date basis. Also rewrote the timeline bar green/red split (slip-aware, handles start pushed past target and a null target), made milestone bars render + draggable, and made the detail-panel risk banner attribute a slip to its real cause. +20 new tests (12 in roadmap-forecast.test.ts, 8 in roadmap-model.test.ts).
summary: Effort-aware, envelope-clamped forecast — no phantom dependency slip

description: For dreamcontext to work across a team, .claude/ and _dream_context/ must be shared together. The brain-only 'separate' mode synced only _dream_context/ into its own dedicated repo and needed the 'platform' symlink hack to drag the Claude Code layer along — architecturally wrong. Cloud sync is now one model: full-repo (whole project → its own origin, current branch) when ON, in-tree (commit-only, never pushes) when OFF. Removed: platform-layer.ts + brain platform command, detach.ts + brain detach, brain init/attach/discover, the create/attach/attach-preview/discover/disconnect/scope server routes + dashboard hooks, dashboard BrainRepoSetup + the two-card scope chooser + i18n keys, and the brainRepo.remote/codeRepoUrl/marker config fields. The master cloud-sync toggle now sets full-repo on enable (400 no_origin without a project origin) and in-tree on disable. No migration — a stale mode:separate config silently resolves to in-tree. Kept (all shared with full-repo): GitHub device-flow + PAT login, GIT_ASKPASS token supply, the scrub gate, the semantic merge agent + /dream-sync, and the GitHubLogin/TeamUpdatesBadge/BrainSyncControl components. Skills + docs swept to the two-mode model. 41 files, +465/−4199 lines.
summary: Removed separate mode — whole-project (full-repo) is the only cloud-sync option

description: Knowledge now supports arbitrary topical subfolders beyond data-structures/diagrams/products. Features (PRDs) can be grouped into topical/product subfolders under knowledge/features/ (e.g. features/lina/, features/memoryos/) — flat and nested features are first-class everywhere (recall corpus, graph, releases, dashboard tree, etc.). A feature's product is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH derived from its top-level folder path (features/lina/checkout.md → lina; features/lina/growth/x.md → lina; features/x.md → undefined/global) — the stored product: frontmatter field was removed across ~24 feature files because it could diverge from the path. Moving a feature (via features move) is the one operation that re-scopes it. New helpers in features-path.ts: featureSlug (folder-qualified slug relative to features/, e.g. lina/checkout), featureProduct (top-level folder only), featureProductFromRelPath (for recall corpus). Recall corpus, graph, initializer, release-discovery/backpopulate all updated to recurse subfolders and emit folder-qualified slugs. New test suite tests/unit/features-subfolder.test.ts pins the invariants. Touches doctor.ts, knowledge.ts, memory.ts, sleep.ts, features-path.ts, initializer-detect.ts, recall.ts, release-backpopulate.ts, release-discovery.ts.
summary: Features support arbitrary topical/product subfolders; product is path-derived (SSOT), never stored

description: The snapshot's 'Connected projects' block now shows each peer's pinned knowledge titles (capped at 5, alphabetically sorted) — drawn from the peer-summary cache so the hot-path stays zero-I/O. New PeerSummary.pinnedKnowledge field; readPinnedTitles() scans both knowledge/** (recursive, flat + migrated features) and core/features/ (un-migrated peers) for pinned: true frontmatter, extracts title ?? name, dedupes, and caps at 5. The glance shows TITLES only (not full content); agents query live via recall --vault <name> when detail is needed. Tazelik semantics same as the rest of the peer-summary cache — refreshes on federation peers sync/connect or next sleep. Decision: peer-pinning a doc does NOT pin it for you (pin is a LOCAL file-level feature). Updated knowledge/federation-cross-vault.md snapshot-glance section.
summary: Snapshot glance surfaces peer-pinned knowledge titles (cap 5, both-scan)

description: Close every remaining gap in whole-project (full-repo) sync: real one-click 'Resolve with AI' (banner launches in-app agent running /dream-sync, browser dashboard falls back to copyable command); detached HEAD detection (refuse, never push a detached HEAD onto main); distinguish user's own git merge from team-merge handoff; code-conflict policy (files outside _dream_context/ defer to human editor, never semantically merged or sent to the agent; report separates code from brain conflicts); classified network/auth/permission/push-rejected failures with concrete recovery steps (Reconnect GitHub / Retry / names the repo+scope / offline-will-retry); scrub-block guidance + one-click add-to-.gitignore (safe local secret files only, hardened against !-negation injection); auto-checkpoint transparency (identifiable + undoable) + a disable-on-open setting; non-fast-forward recovery (Retry sync); mandatory pre-push scrub over the FULL push range in both auto and --push-only. Sidebar sync UI extracted to BrainSyncControl.tsx; status route adds mergeKind + codeConflicts. Full suite 3013 green.
summary: Cloud sync hardening: one-click AI resolve, full-repo safety, classified failure recovery

description: New skill/references/brain-sync.md: three sync modes (separate/full-repo/in-tree), setup, secrets-first token auth (no gh/credential-helper) + required push scope, shared-vs-machine-local config, lifecycle, cross-OS setup (per-machine token, Windows CRLF + symlink privilege, enclosing-repo trap, WSL), silent-failure troubleshooting, second-machine onboarding. Slimmed integrations.md to a pointer + added full-repo; documented sleep-done brain sync in sleep.md; repointed SKILL.md; 6 marker tests pin the load-bearing sections. Closes the doc lag behind the full-repo hardening code (d8f633d).

description: Two dashboard controls: (1) a refresh button next to the sidebar's Synced badge runs a full brain pull+merge (POST /api/brain/sync mode:auto) with transient outcome feedback — success check, 'awaiting /dream-sync' warning on a deferred team merge, scrub-block warning; (2) Settings → Memory gains the recall-mode control (haiku / raw / hybrid / off) that was missing for v0.14.0's experimental hybrid recall — hybrid carries an Experimental badge and the ~113MB first-use model-download note; PATCH /api/sleep now accepts a validated recall_mode.

description: Platform layer: 'dreamcontext brain platform' moves CLAUDE.md + .claude into _dream_context/platform/ and symlinks them back from the project root, so a separate-mode brain repo shares the whole Claude Code layer (skills, agents, hooks) with the team. Every brain sync re-creates missing root symlinks (fresh clones self-wire), doctor flags broken links/conflicts, and the brain gitignore gains platform machine-local excludes (settings.local.json, scheduled_tasks.lock) + **/.DS_Store. Hardening: setupPlatformLayer writes the full canonical gitignore first — a platform-only stub used to defeat bootstrapBrainRepo's !existsSync guard and could sync local merge locks.
summary: brain platform: CLAUDE.md + .claude sync with the brain repo via platform/ + root symlinks

description: Ships the embedding epic tasks 1-4 (decision-embedding-layer): multilingual-e5-small via @huggingface/transformers (optionalDependency, ~113MB one-time download, models cached at ~/.dreamcontext/models), heading-boundary code-fence-aware chunker, content-hash incremental vector cache at _dream_context/.embeddings/ (self-gitignoring, mtime+size pre-filter, --force bypass), confidence-adaptive fusion (relative-score when BM25 decisive, weighted RRF when weak, top-1 pin guard, changelog docs excluded from dense channel). Raw BM25 score untouched in every mode — hook gates frozen. Freshness: lazy per hybrid query, eager --force at sleep done (gated on existing cache), plus new 'dreamcontext embed refresh [--force|--if-present]' and 'embed status' commands. A/B on frozen gold sets: train r@1 76.7→81.7, held-out 60.0→66.7, held-out r@5 90→96.7, turkish r@1 doubled, EN paraphrase r@1 +16.7, zero regressed recall/MRR cells; all four graduation gates met. Off by default; degrades to plain BM25 without the model. 41 new tests; suite 2922 green.
summary: v0.14.0: experimental local embedding layer — opt-in hybrid BM25+dense recall (dreamcontext recall hybrid), multilingual, fully offline

description: Claude Code rotates the live conversation UUID under a running tab (/clear, resume picker); tabs were pinned to their birth conversation UUID, breaking auto-title (renamed the wrong tab from a stale transcript), resume (stale snapshots), stats, model picker. Fix: PTY exports DREAMCONTEXT_TAB_SESSION=<tab id>; SessionStart hook records tab→live-conversation-id into state/.agent-session-map/ (per-tab sidecar files, gitignored incl. team-brain gitignore); server resolves through the map (src/lib/agent-session-map.ts, exported UUID_RE, liveTranscriptPath() helper) for --resume, auto-title, session stats, model picker. Nested-claude guard: isNestedClaudeHook() walks ps ancestry, skips recording for claude -p runs inside a tab, fails open on Windows.

description: The Sleepy 'Sleep' agent's initial prompt was typed client-side after a ~1600ms boot settle timer. On slow boots (MCP auth delays), readline wasn't ready → timer fired into silence → text dropped → Sleep agent stuck on empty prompt. Fix: auto-submit (Sleep) mode passes the prompt as a POSITIONAL arg to claude via new prompt URL param in agent-terminal.ts (injection-safe, mirrors generateTitle pattern). Claude boots with the message already submitted — timing race eliminated. Fragile client-side injection now only remains for write-but-don't-send composer skill-add case.
summary: Replaced fragile readline-injection with positional-arg prompt for Sleepy auto-submit

description: Lab (Insights) skill docs shipped FOR REAL this time, plus a new Entity Router. Discovery: the v0.11.0 Lab skill docs were never committed to skill/ — the edits hit only the installed .claude/skills/ copies and were clobbered by the next update, while the task changelog falsely claimed 'skill docs updated'. Consequence: agents misrouted 'create insight' to knowledge create. Fix (class-level, not just patching the gap): (1) Lab/Insights row added to the SKILL.md Capabilities-at-a-Glance table; (2) new 'Entity Router' section in SKILL.md disambiguating all 8 entity types (task/feature/knowledge/insight/objective/memory/bookmark/data-structure) so create-X requests route to the right command; (3) full Lab reference sections written into skill/references/tasks-and-features.md, cli-reference.md, and knowledge-and-recall.md; (4) marker tests (tests/unit/taxonomy-markers.test.ts) now pin the Lab doc content so a future clobber fails CI instead of silently regressing. Rule reinforced: skill docs are edited in skill/ (source of truth) and verified with git — never in installed copies.
summary: Lab skill docs actually committed to skill/ + new 8-entity Entity Router in SKILL.md; marker tests now pin the Lab docs (v0.11.0 docs were silently lost to installed-copy edits)

description: High-effort adversarial review over the Lab working tree (8 finder angles + 12 verifiers, recall-biased per the user-corrected protocol: 3+5 angles × 6 candidates → 1-vote verify → ≤10 findings) produced 10 verified findings, all fixed: (1) InsightDetailPanel — Escape now routes through the overlayStack and an input-focus guard prevents Escape-in-input closing the panel; (2) LineChart — stale hoverIdx clamped where it is READ against the current xKeys (props can shrink between renders), touch-action pan-y, memoization; (3) InsightCard — keyboard access (focus/enter/space); (4) lab sync — tolerates malformed persisted history and truncates stored error strings to 300 chars (a count-capped list of unbounded strings still bloats), with 2 regression tests; (5) PieChart — lone slice renders as a full circle instead of a degenerate arc; (6) SKILL.md rule 3 + cli-reference — stale recall enumerations refreshed. Full suite 2838 green.
summary: Adversarial review of Lab working tree: 10 verified fixes (Escape/overlayStack, hoverIdx clamp, a11y, sync history tolerance + error truncation, PieChart lone slice, stale docs) — 2838 tests green

description: After v0.11.0 shipped, main went red on three CI-environment issues despite local green. (1) d4e9fee: build the CLI before tests — integration suite spawns the compiled CLI at dist/index.js, but ci.yml ran only npm ci → npm test with no build step; dist/ is produced by prepublishOnly (publish-only), so CI had no CLI → ~20 integration files failed ENOENT. Also dropped hardcoded dev paths in excalidraw-knowledge.test.ts. (2) 205e820: configure git defaultBranch=main + identity fallback, and make marketing-hooks e2e hermetic — a fresh runner has no init.defaultBranch, so brain-sync.e2e's bare repo HEAD pointed at master (not main) → second clone came up empty; the marketing-hooks 'blocks a commit' test resolved the GLOBAL dreamcontext (not the built one) and failed open when absent. (3) f4ee5a3: switch to vitest forks pool + capped concurrency=2 on CI to kill spurious 'Timeout calling onTaskUpdate' — a few CPU-bound files (recall-capture-stress, recall-eval) run tens of seconds; under the default thread pool on a contended runner they starved the worker→main RPC past its timeout. Process IPC (forks) is serviced by libuv, not the blocked event loop. Full suite 224 files / 2801 tests now pass on CI.
summary: Three CI hardening commits: vitest forks pool + git config + hermetic marketing-hooks e2e + CLI build step

description: Resolve the GitHub device-flow client_id LIVE (resolveBrainOAuthClientId) instead of freezing it at module load, so the desktop main process can inject DREAMCONTEXT_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID after module load (the Tauri thin-shell spawns the Node server with env vars the import-time const never sees). isOAuthAppConfigured detects the shipped placeholder client_id ('Iv23xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx' — GitHub 404s unregistered client_ids) and short-circuits the device flow to the PAT path with a 501 + actionable error, preserving the M2 security posture (desktop gate, server-only device_code, 0600 token, never echoed to client). The placeholder gate ensures production never leaks the real client_id while the OQ-1 registration task is pending. 19/19 touched tests pass, reviewer PASS.

description: Add a comprehensive analytics-insights subsystem that lets agents stay current on metrics without manual syncing. Users define named INSIGHTS (curated metrics, never raw data dumps) backed by external sources: any HTTP JSON API (generic adapter with GET/POST, URL fidelity guarded, credential redaction, POST body single-encoded) or a custom script (.mjs async fn). One CLI sync refreshes all or one; cached snapshots live in lab/cache/ so every session sees them (SessionStart snapshot + recall type 'insight'). A bound roadmap Key Result metric updates automatically on sync — upgrading the roadmap from PO-asserted numbers to measured numbers. Core files: src/lib/lab/store.ts (manifest read/write, isSafeInsightSlug), credentials.ts (gitignore-first 0600 write, secret redaction, readCredentials), sync.ts (syncInsight skips fresh, --force, failed[], KR auto-write via updateObjectiveMetric), rollup.ts (deriveGranularity monthly@365/weekly@120/daily@30, MAX_POINTS rollup), tweaks.ts (resolveTweaks: enum last_1_year → ~365d window + from/to date override), adapters/http.ts (ApiAdapter buildUrl trailing-slash/query-corruption fix, fetchImpl injection, seriesPath split), adapters/script.ts (loadScript default async fn). CLI: dreamcontext lab create/list/show/sync/tweak/credentials (set/list). Dashboard: Lab page (LabPage.tsx, useLab.ts), server routes (GET /api/lab list summaries, GET /api/lab/:slug full series, POST /api/lab/sync {all:true}, PATCH /api/lab/:slug/tweaks), no route ever returns a credential value. doctor checkLab: WARN on credentials_used key absent, FAIL on credentials.json exists but gitignoreCovers() false (self-healing net), STUB-REGRESSION GUARD on writeCredential (produces FULL canonical brain gitignore, not a 2-line stub). Script-hash tripwire: loud notice on changed script before executing. Gitignored: lab/cache/, lab/credentials.json. 9 unit tests (lab-adapter-http, lab-adapter-script, lab-credentials, lab-doctor, lab-recall, lab-rollup, lab-store, lab-sync, lab-tweaks, server-lab-routes); all acceptance criteria in task frontmatter. In-progress work (uncommitted, not all criteria green yet).
summary: Lab analytics-insights subsystem — insight store, adapters (HTTP/script), CLI sync, dashboard Lab page, roadmap KR auto-binding (in-progress)

description: The desktop app's Launcher route (full-screen FederationBoard) was rendered outside the I18nProvider wrapper in App.tsx, so any component using the useI18n hook would crash with 'useI18n must be used within I18nContext.Provider'. Fixed by ensuring every route, including the Launcher, is wrapped in I18nProvider. The FederationBoard's ~50 new i18n keys now resolve correctly in the Launcher window.
summary: Launcher window missing I18nProvider — anything using useI18n crashed

description: d3-zoom's pan gesture binds to 'mousedown', not 'pointerdown'. When the FederationBoard's custom card-drag gesture cancelled the 'pointerdown' event but let the browser emit the compatibility 'mousedown', d3-zoom interpreted the mouse event as a pan-start and moved the canvas underneath the user's card drag. Fixed by also cancelling/swallowing the compatibility mousedown event in the card's onMouseDown handler (stopPropagation + preventDefault). This is the load-bearing pattern for any custom drag atop d3-zoom: you must cancel both the pointer event you claim AND the compat mouse event the browser emits afterward, because d3-zoom pans on the mouse event.
summary: d3-zoom pan-gesture interaction — cancel compat mousedown when claiming pointer event

description: When a FederationBoard had no wires between cards (unconnected vaults), the strong repulsion force (charge -900) scattered them toward the canvas corners, and auto-fit zoom pulled back until they were tiny specks. Fixed by softening the charge to -350, adding forceX/forceY(0) center-pull + forceCollide(85) based on card footprint, and clamping auto-fit zoom to [0.9, 1.4] so cards always stay readable. Minimal d3-force-3d type definitions added (dashboard/src/types/d3-force-3d.d.ts) for the subset of the API the board uses. Verified with a fresh screenshot: five unconnected cards now sit in a compact, legible cluster mid-canvas.
summary: Unlinked federation graph cards no longer scatter to unreadable specks

description: Roadmap objectives now surface deeper context in every agent briefing: a distilled description (first prose line from the body), impact/effort scores, start_date, and auto-derived slip attribution (slip_days numeric + slip_upstream pointing to the responsible dependency, else own tasks). Milestone-only objectives (no dated tasks of their own) inherit forecast from max(dependency forecast_end) so pure coordination milestones slip when upstream slips. Snapshot and SubagentStart briefings now order active objectives by time-box relevance (this month → this quarter → later; overdue first) and render impact/effort + slip cause ('Nd late · upstream: dep-slug' / 'own tasks'). sleep-tasks may now refresh an objective's metric.current via 'roadmap objective metric <slug> --current' (reverses the 2026-07-04 sleep-write ban for that one field; all other objective fields stay PO-owned). dreamcontext-deep-research recall sweeps now include --types objective. New 'Proactive objective capture' protocol: on a stated or implied outcome, the in-session agent dedups via recall, OFFERS an objective, ASKS dates, OFFERS a Key Result metric, and DETECTS+PROPOSES dependencies — every write gated on a yes (objectives stay PO-authored; ask-first, never silent). Dashboard roadmap components (board/detail panel) thread description, slip_days, slip_upstream through the model and render them inline. 6 new roadmap-model unit tests; full suite 2610 green.
summary: Objectives gain richer snapshot presence — description, impact/effort, slip attribution, milestone forecast
