description: Adds a SleepDebtTracker widget to the right side of the header: a linear progress bar climbing toward the 'Must Sleep' ceiling (debt/20), paired with the coded, mood-animated SleepyMascot face. Both are driven by the same live /api/sleep debt (polls every 15s via useSleep), so rest state tracks in near real time. Level colours the fill (green→violet→amber→red+pulse) and the face mood (awake→drowsy→asleep). Click opens the full Sleep page. Also realigns the frontend debt thresholds in useSleep.ts to the canonical source (src/lib/sleep-consolidation.ts): the backend rescaled x2 to 8/14/20 on 2026-06-29 but the frontend was left on the old 4/7/10 scale. This corrects getSleepLevel / getSleepLevelKey / getSleepMood (also consumed by SleepPage) and exports SLEEP_DEBT_MAX.
summary: Live sleep-debt tracker + animated Sleepy face in header, real-time debt monitoring

description: Resolves the monetization open question on the make-it-a-business objective: open-source free CLI as the distribution channel, closed-source paid desktop app as the product ($9.99/mo, 14-day trial). Key positioning is zero data custody — team collaboration runs over the customer's own GitHub, so context never touches our servers and we carry no data liability. Adds an MRR key result (target $2000) so the outcome is tracked, not just described. This is a significant product-direction decision by the PO.
summary: Define 'Make it a Business' — free CLI + paid closed-source app, zero data custody

description: Brain lives in its own GitHub repo; git-illiterate teammates stay in sync automatically. Validated plan v3.2+v3.3 (3 reviewers, 5 iterations, 21 findings) + 3-round code review (PASS) + validation (PASS). New lib/git-sync/ module family: git wrapper (GitSyncError, --no-commit merges), scrub gate (BLOCK/WARN, redacted excerpts), GIT_ASKPASS credential supply (token never in URL/argv/env; 0600 tmp file), deterministic semantic merge (changelog/releases/config/task union rules; prose defers to agent), conflict-report handoff, sync engine (5-clause reentrancy guard, auto/pull-only/push-only, --resume/--continue, post-merge re-scrub, PID-liveness brain lock, C4 non-FF retry). CLI commands: dreamcontext brain {status,enable,disable,init,attach,discover,scrub,sync} (private-by-default init, trust-gated attach, master switch with GitHub-connected derived default). Sleep done auto-sync (best-effort-but-loud) + session-start detached pull-only spawn (PATH-safe, DREAMCONTEXT_BRAIN_SYNC=0 kill switch). Includes /dream-sync skill (skill-sync/) with merge-rules reference, installed as core skill. file-lock: opt-in verifyPidLiveness (existing callers unchanged). 96 new tests incl. 8-scenario real-git E2E; suite 2604 green, build clean.
summary: M1 — GitHub brain-repo cloud sync CLI core: git-illiterate teammates auto-sync via sleep

description: Product Owner replaced the three placeholder objectives (increase-retention-20, launch-mobile-app, ship-v0-2-3) with six real strategic outcomes in core/objectives/: improve-recall-mechanism (target 2026-08-16), improve-sleep-quality (2026-08-02), include-codex-opencode-support (2026-08-23), make-dreamcontext-team-ready (2026-07-12, one task in progress), simplified-ux (2026-07-31), and hello-world-pr (2026-09-19, depends on the first four). This is the roadmap foundation for the next 2-3 months of work. Objectives are PO-authored markdown files; agents propose task linkage but never edit the objectives themselves.
summary: PO repopulated objectives/ with 6 real outcomes, replacing 3 placeholder objectives

description: Adds a ⌘P "Go to Project" switcher available in every window (in-place vault hop / launcher-focus, ⌘1-9 quick jumps) and extracts the scrim/dialog/keyboard chrome it shares with the ⌘K palette into a reusable <CommandModal> + useListKeyboardNav hook, backed by a LIFO overlay stack so Esc always closes the front-most overlay. CommandModal(.tsx/.css) provides shared scrim + centered dialog with entrance animation + reduced-motion support, overlay-stack registration, and topmost-aware Esc handling (capture-phase, stopImmediatePropagation). CommandPalette refactored onto the shell with zero behavior/visual change; retains .command-palette class for the AgentSurface Esc guard. overlayStack.ts is a push/pop/isTop LIFO registry. Shell memoizes palette onClose so it can't re-assert itself topmost on every re-render. desktop.ts adds goToProject/openLauncherHome/switchVaultInPlace; launcher status poll now gated to when the switcher is open.
summary: ⌘P project switcher + shared CommandModal shell for ⌘K and ⌘P, backed by LIFO overlay stack for correct Esc behavior

description: The `model: sonnet` alias now resolves to Sonnet 5, whose heavy default adaptive thinking slowed RemSleep consolidation. Pinned all five sleep specialists (tasks/state/product/federation/migration) to claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 for consistent, faster execution. Per-subagent effort/thinking isn't configurable in Claude Code, and Sonnet 4.5 doesn't support the `effort` API param, so instead of raising sleep-tasks to Opus we added a targeted "think hard" instruction to its completed-vs-in_review judgment step. Ships to users on next `dreamcontext update` (core refresh overwrites .claude/agents unconditionally).
summary: Pin all five sleep specialists to Sonnet 4.5 (claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929) + think-hard nudge on sleep-tasks

description: Worked through the cross-machine/multi-person brain-collaboration design with the user (Turkish-language session). Confirmed architecture: the dreamcontext brain (_dream_context/) lives in its OWN GitHub repo, separate from the code repo it documents — for authorization separation (share the brain without the code or vice versa), noise separation (automatic sleep commits shouldn't pollute the code repo's PR diffs/history), and pointer clarity (the brain repo records the code repo's URL + expected layout; the launcher guides a new machine through cloning the code repo and then building a LOCAL-ONLY index — indexes are machine-derived and are never pushed, restored via .gitignore + rebuild-on-clone). D0 (first shippable) scope is READ-ONLY: GitHub login via device-flow OAuth (PAT as fallback, token in macOS keychain), list-and-clone accessible dreamcontext-brain repos (marked via a GitHub topic or a root marker file), point at the paired code repo, local indexing — zero push risk. A manual '/dream-sync'-style skill and true automatic post-sleep git sync (fetch, then an AGENT — not a git client — performs a semantic merge: union changelogs, content-merge knowledge files, keep the more-advanced task status, then commit+push) are later phases, explicitly sequenced after the read-only phase because agent-driven semantic merge is the hardest and most valuable part of the plan. A secrets/absolute-local-path scrub gate must run before any automatic push (env vars, API keys, and local paths can leak into brain files during a session). Personal-layer question (user.md / episodic preferences are person-specific, must not be pushed shared or must be namespaced per person) reuses the existing multi-person people[] roster rather than inventing new per-person namespacing — left as the open design question to resolve before Phase 2 (opt for never-pushed-personal vs namespaced-and-portable). task-backend/github.ts (already shipped for issue sync) is reused for future issue-mapping rather than building a new integration. Decision and reasoning captured for continuity; no code shipped this session.
summary: Design decision: brain lives in its own GitHub repo (separate from code), with post-sleep git sync gated behind a manual sync skill, agent-driven semantic merge, and a local-only personal layer

description: New POST /api/agent/title {claudeId} reads the session's transcript file (~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<uuid>.jsonl — resumable sessions are already tracked this way), extracts the first real user message (skipping tool-result/reminder lines), and asks 'claude --model haiku -p' for a Title Case, punctuation-free label of at most 6 words, run from the home directory so the project's SessionStart brain-preload never fires. AgentSurface fires this exactly once per session, on the busy-to-idle edge after the first turn completes, and only renames a tab that still holds its default 'Agent N' name — a manual rename always wins even mid-flight. New GET/POST /api/launcher/agent-settings persists the Agents-surface preferences (enabled, restoreTabs, defaultAgent, the new autoTitle, hotkey) to ~/.dreamcontext/agent-ui.json since the app's per-launch loopback port resets localStorage; SettingsPage gained an 'Auto-name tabs from your first message' toggle (default on) alongside the existing Agents section. AgentTabs and agentSession.ts also picked up incidental keybinding/rendering polish from the same pass. Verified end-to-end live (bad-UUID 400, no-transcript null, and a crafted transcript producing a correct 3-word title).
summary: Agent terminal tabs auto-name themselves from your first message via a single Haiku call, plus a persisted Agents-settings toggle and terminal keybinding polish

description: Root-caused and fixed six issues surfaced while validating the new Cmd+P ProjectSwitcher against the mature Cmd+K CommandPalette it reuses. New dashboard/src/lib/overlayStack.ts is a tiny LIFO registry (push/pop/isTopOverlay) so each modal's Escape handler only fires when it is the topmost open overlay — previously Cmd+K open then Cmd+P then Esc closed the wrong palette because both handlers listened on window in capture phase with no ordering contract. Also fixed: every vault window polled /api/launcher/status every 15s regardless of whether its window was open (useLauncherStatus now gates on an enabled flag, mirroring the existing useRecall(open?) pattern); Escape only closed a palette while an input had focus, now a window-level capture-phase handler closes regardless of focus; Cmd+P could fire while typing inside the embedded agent terminal, now guarded by isTerminalTarget excluding .xterm/.agent-surface; copy-to-clipboard dropped its prefers-reduced-motion guard. Extracted a shared <VaultDot> component (launcher + switcher previously had three near-duplicate status-dot implementations) and a single DRAG_EXEMPT_SELECTOR + isDragExempt helper in desktop.ts replacing three inline copies. Deferred as debt: a shared <CommandModal> shell to de-duplicate ~180 lines of near-identical CSS/keyboard-list logic between Cmd+K and Cmd+P — flagged for its own review-scoped refactor rather than folding into this bug-fix pass.
summary: Cmd+P Project Switcher hardened: overlayStack LIFO fixes Esc-closes-wrong-palette, plus 5 more findings from a root-cause review (polling leak, terminal key-leak, Esc-not-window-level, shared VaultDot/drag-exempt helpers)

description: The header Update badge now performs the upgrade instead of just describing it. POST /api/launcher/upgrade spawns the same 'dreamcontext upgrade --yes' cascade the CLI exposes inside an interactive login shell ($SHELL -ilc, so npm/npx resolve even when the app was launched from Finder) as a singleton background job; GET /api/launcher/upgrade/status polls state (idle/running/done/error) plus a tailed stdout+stderr log capped at 8000 chars. A new POST /api/launcher/relaunch detaches a 'sleep 2 && open <app>' into its own process group (escaping the parent-death reaper) so the app can quit and reopen itself with the freshly installed bundle. UpdateBadge.tsx restores in-flight or just-finished runs on remount so a closed-and-reopened popover never re-POSTs a redundant upgrade. version-check.ts now reports currentCli/latestCli/cliOutdated independent of the prose-nudge suppression, so the desktop app can always offer the one-click action even when the CLI-only text nudge is hidden. Code review (session 236e8894) caught two correctness gaps, both fixed in this pass: applyStatus lacked a branch to stop polling on a lost/idle server-side job, and the upgrade POST route reported state:'running' even when the child spawn failed synchronously — both addressed before landing.
summary: In-app one-click 'Upgrade everything': header badge now runs dreamcontext upgrade --yes end-to-end (CLI + desktop app + every registered project) with live log + relaunch

description: Shipped dreamcontext@0.10.6 to npm (OTP-gated publish) and as a GitHub desktop .app release, closing out the v0.10.5 publish workflow. Root-cause investigation confirmed the v0.10.5 npm tarball already contained the OKR roadmap dashboard live (identical dashboard bundle hash index-DalMBSek.js, 24 roadmap references) — the failure on a second machine was stale-install caching, not a missing build: npx cache plus the desktop app's manifest-based skip-redownload-when-version-matches logic (src/cli/commands/app.ts) both short-circuit on an unchanged version string. Bumping all five version surfaces (package.json, desktop/package.json, tauri.conf.json, Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock) to 0.10.6 forces every install path to fetch fresh with no source delta. 2492 tests green, pack dry-run correct, v0.10.6 tag pushed triggering desktop-release CI (build, ad-hoc sign, package, verify, publish). The v0.10.5 GitHub desktop release (aarch64 .app.tar.gz + .sha256) was published separately as Latest before the 0.10.6 bump.
summary: Publish dreamcontext@0.10.6 to npm + GitHub desktop release — cache-busting republish that forces stale installs to pull the v0.10.5 roadmap payload

description: Shipped the interactive dashboard surface for the OKR objective board (a94b1e8): a Roadmap page with a forecast timeline (month-gridded axis, gradient status bars, dotted target diamonds, red slip-overshoot hatching, bezier dependency connectors that redden on cascade slip), a live forecast cascade computed client-side (frontend topo/finish-to-start) so dragging a bar to reschedule slides and reddens every dependent's forecast live while only the dragged objective's dates actually persist, drag-from-node to link a dependency with hover-X to unlink, a status-column board view, and a slide-over ObjectiveDetailPanel that is fully inline-editable (title, status with clear-override, committed start/target via a new DateRangePicker, Impact x Effort, dependencies via a new DependencyPicker) with immediate persistence. Backend: GET /api/roadmap (buildRoadmapModel), PATCH /api/objectives/:slug, and cycle-guarded POST/DELETE /api/objectives/:slug/dependencies (src/server/routes/objectives.ts). objectives-store.ts hardened for hand-edited YAML: date coercion on read plus delete self-heal with unhealed-reference reporting. Tasks board gained a task-to-objective assignment control (TaskDetailPanel). Docs: README Roadmap section and DEEP-DIVE version history/what's-next updated; RELEASES.json v0.10.5 summary extended to cover both the OKR board and this dashboard.
summary: Interactive dashboard Roadmap page: forecast timeline with live drag-to-reschedule cascade, drag-to-link dependencies, board view, and a fully inline-editable detail panel

description: Fix Agent terminal rows overflowing past the viewport and clipping mid-word at the right edge. The .xterm rule's text-rendering: optimizeLegibility enabled kerning and ligatures, which breaks the FitAddon contract on a monospace grid: column count is computed from a single glyph's advance, but the DOM renderer then draws each row wider than cols×cellWidth once kerning is applied. Replaced with text-rendering: optimizeSpeed plus explicit font-kerning: none, font-variant-ligatures: none, and font-feature-settings 'liga' 0/'calt' 0, forcing a uniform per-glyph advance while keeping the softer font smoothing from the prior readability polish.
summary: Fix Agent terminal text clipping at the right edge caused by kerning/ligatures breaking the monospace grid

description: Implemented task_uO60nZRt MVP + owner-promoted scope. New: core/objectives/<slug>.md store (CRUD via 'roadmap objective', write-time circular-dep guard, delete self-healing), pure buildRoadmapModel (topo-sorted full-DAG cascade, null-forecast rule, rollups from real status enum, computed reverse edges), 'dreamcontext roadmap' (text board + auto-generated knowledge/roadmap/board.md) and 'roadmap --json' (typed query surface — markdown stays the store, JSON is the query layer per owner decision). Tasks carry local-only 'objectives: [a,b]' (create --objectives, tasks objectives verb, list --objective filter, PATCH allowedFields). Objectives are a first-class recall type (buildCorpus defaults + --types objective), render in the SessionStart snapshot (budget-aware, active + recently-finished + per-task inline) and the SubagentStart briefing. Sleep: sleep-tasks proposes links for EMPTY objectives lists only (PO override never overwritten); flow regenerates the board deterministically. Doctor validates the whole graph. Prompt layer updated end-to-end: SKILL.md + 4 references, sleep/explore/initializer/curator agents + codex mirrors, goal-skill Phase 3, taxonomy aliases, README. 25 new unit tests; full suite green.
summary: OKR roadmap shipped: PO-authored objectives, many-to-many task links, full-DAG forecast cascade, target-vs-forecast slip detection, snapshot/recall/sleep integration

description: Dropping a file onto a split Agent terminal now routes to the pane the cursor is actually over (resolved via document.elementFromPoint into the .agent-pane-slot[data-pane] under the drop point), falling back to the action-focused pane if the point isn't over any pane — previously every drop always went to the last-focused pane regardless of where you dropped. The server route (agent-drop.ts) no longer rejects non-image uploads with a 415: any file type is accepted, magic-byte-sniffed for a canonical extension when it is an image, saved verbatim under the vault's gitignored temp dir otherwise, and its path is injected into the target session so Claude can read code, text, PDFs, and more by path. The existing 25MB per-chunk cap, basename sanitize, and TTL prune are unchanged.
summary: Agent terminal drag-drop now targets the pane under the cursor and accepts any file type, not just images

description: Selection in the embedded Agent terminal is now unmistakable regardless of theme or terminal focus state. The prior selectionBackground was a pale, semi-transparent violet that was nearly invisible on the light-theme white background; worse, xterm draws selection via its separate selectionInactiveBackground (a faint default) whenever the terminal isn't the focused DOM element, which is exactly when the light-mode bug appeared. Fix: pin both selectionBackground and selectionInactiveBackground to a solid #6a57d6 with white selectionForeground (readXtermTheme() in agentSession.ts) — roughly 5.3:1 white-on-violet, clearly visible on white and dark backgrounds whether or not the pane is focused.
summary: Fix Agent terminal text selection: solid brand-violet + white text for both focused and unfocused selection, in both themes

description: Removed the opacity:0.82 + --color-bg overlay that dimmed the non-active pane in a split Agent terminal view (AgentTerminal.css). Users read both terminals at once in a split, so greying out the other pane read as 'blurring it out' rather than useful focus signaling. The active pane's accent ring and top bar are sufficient to mark which pane has focus; both panes now render at full legibility regardless of which is active.
summary: Remove Agent terminal inactive-split-pane dimming — both panes stay fully legible, accent ring alone marks focus

description: Cmd+C and Cmd+X in the embedded Agent terminal now copy the current selection themselves via a hidden-textarea + document.execCommand('copy') (copyPreservingUnicode() in agentSession.ts), and preventDefault the keydown so WKWebView no longer rings the macOS system beep on an otherwise-unhandled Cmd-key. The hidden-textarea path also fixes an encoding bug: navigator.clipboard.writeText() in this WKWebView re-decodes UTF-8 bytes as Mac Roman, mangling any non-ASCII character copied out of the terminal (e.g. c-cedilla, g-breve, arrows); execCommand routes through the OS's native copy pipeline and round-trips correctly. Cmd+A now selects the whole buffer (also swallowed to avoid the beep). Cmd+V is deliberately left untouched — xterm's native bracketed paste must stay intact or a multi-line paste would auto-submit each line early.
summary: Fix Agent terminal copy/cut: encoding-safe clipboard copy via hidden-textarea+execCommand, and stop the macOS system beep

description: Switch the embedded Agent terminal (agentSession.ts) from xterm's WebGL renderer to its DOM renderer: WebGL rasterizes its own glyph atlas and ignores -webkit-font-smoothing, producing hard/'sharp' edges that the user found eye-tiring; the DOM renderer draws real text nodes and gets native macOS anti-aliasing. Soften the foreground from a stark #f5f6fa (~17:1 contrast) to #cdd3de (dark) / #33383f (light), and lower minimumContrastRatio 4.5->3 so xterm's contrast floor still rescues unreadable ANSI block-fills without flattening dim/secondary text bright. Bump fontSize 13.5->14.5 and lineHeight 1.4->1.65 for a calmer, fuller feel, and add '.xterm { -webkit-font-smoothing: auto }' to override the app-wide CSS reset that force-thinned every terminal glyph to the antialiased (thinnest) mode. Along the way, found and fixed a bonus bug: the intended 'Sometype Mono' font was never actually loaded (no @font-face; index.html only pulled JetBrains Mono@400), so terminal text had silently been rendering as JetBrains Mono 400 with faux-synthesized bold the whole time. JetBrains Mono is now the loaded --font-mono primary with real 400/500/700 weights via Google Fonts.
summary: Agent terminal readability overhaul: DOM renderer (native AA) replaces WebGL, softened fg + minContrast 4.5->3, size/line-height tuned, and JetBrains Mono now actually loads with real weights

description: v0.10.2 bumped the version across the full monorepo surface (package.json, desktop/package.json, tauri.conf.json, Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock), realigning desktop/package.json which had drifted to 0.9.2 during the 0.10.x desktop-only bumps. No code delta over v0.10.1 — this release exists purely to get npm and the desktop bundle back on the same version number, and was the first npm publish since v0.10.0.
summary: v0.10.2 shipped: version-sync only — desktop surfaces realigned to 0.10.2, first npm publish since v0.10.0

description: v0.10.1 tagged and published as a desktop-app release (GitHub Release with the aarch64 .app.tar.gz + .sha256 asset, built by desktop-release.yml). Ships: the in-app Agent surface that superseded the Sleepy dashboard page (multi-session split-pane terminals, Cmd-D drag-to-split, Cmd-T/Cmd-W, minimize-to-corner dock, detached-DOM persistence), a Cmd-K command palette over live recall (BM25 + Haiku toggle), image-drop-to-vault injection, an in-app prerequisite installer for the Claude CLI/node-pty, a readable ANSI ramp (contrast 4.5), and macOS line-editing keybindings in the Agent terminal. Also carries the board version smart-buckets (Current/Backlog/Completed), the multi-assignee AvatarStack, the atomic sleep-consolidation mutex with rescaled debt thresholds, timeline-gantt polish, and the 404-on-missing-static-asset fix.
summary: v0.10.1 shipped: desktop app release train — in-app Agent surface, ⌘K palette, smart-bucket board filter, AvatarStack, sleep mutex

description: Ran the full pre-publish workflow (last run at v0.9.2; v0.10.0/0.10.1/0.10.2 had shipped as quick version bumps without it, letting docs/diagrams/RELEASES.json drift and letting the q030 capture-guard regression ship unnoticed). Refreshed README.md and DEEP-DIVE.md against everything the release actually ships (in-app Agent surface, curator skill/agents, version rename/delete, GitHub image bridge, board smart-buckets/AvatarStack, CI); rebuilt all 8 Excalidraw diagram figures (architecture, council, federation, how-it-works, neuroscience, problem, recall, sleep) via the excalidraw build pipeline, clearing the content-drift flag carried on the council and federation boards from prior sleep cycles; rewrote docs/PRE-PUBLISH-TEST-PLAN.md end to end. RELEASES.json backfilled summaries for v0.10.1 and v0.10.2 (published to npm but previously undocumented).
summary: Refresh README/DEEP-DIVE and rebuild all 8 Excalidraw diagram figures + PRE-PUBLISH-TEST-PLAN for the v0.10.5 pre-publish workflow

description: ensureClaudeHooks() deduped managed hooks by (command, matcher) and only added a hook when absent, so it never updated the timeout of an already-installed one — a project whose settings.json was written by a pre-120 installer (back when recall's UserPromptSubmit hook was pinned at 5s) kept timing out forever, even after upgrading dreamcontext and re-running install, because Haiku-backed recall needs more than 5s. Fixed by reconciling an existing managed hook's timeout to the canonical spec value on every install/upgrade run (Codex's installer already self-healed this way since it rewrites its whole managed block each run); the install summary now surfaces which hook timeouts were healed. Regression test added alongside the existing 'migrates old snapshot hook' coverage.
summary: Fix install-skill: reconcile an existing hook's stale timeout to the current spec instead of leaving it forever

description: Upstreamed a fix (previously applied in another vault) to the canonical skill-packs/excalidraw/ generator: card() now calls a new fitText helper (glyph-metric wrap + shrink-to-fit) so a label can never overflow its box, and connector() gains via waypoints with elbow right-angle routing plus arc-length label placement so an arrow no longer cuts through an intervening box. The glyph-metric sizing helper was mirrored into build_excalidraw.js's sizeText(), and all copies (skill-packs/excalidraw, the git-tracked scripts/diagrams/excalidraw duplicate, and the gitignored .claude/.agents/dist runtime mirrors) were re-synced to stay byte-identical. SKILL.md documents the new auto-fit and routing behavior for future board generators.
summary: Excalidraw skill: auto-fit card text so it can't overflow the box, and route connectors around intervening boxes

description: Three related defects in the self-contained board-folder convention (a board plus a sibling .teardown.md/notes, with images under an assets/ subfolder). Bug A: buildKnowledgeTree's board-self-wrapper-collapse heuristic unconditionally hoisted a board out of its folder, which was correct only for a lone board but split the folder when a sibling card (or a nested notes subfolder) shared it; fixed by gating the collapse on a subtree-wide occupant count (every ancestor prefix), not just direct children. Bug B1: the /api/knowledge-assets/:slug embedded-image resolver tried the board folder and an Attachments/ subfolder but not the assets/ subfolder the self-contained convention actually uses, so every embed rendered blank; added boardDir/<path> and boardDir/assets/<basename> candidates, both still passing the existing safeChildPath containment guard (verified tighter, not looser, than the pre-existing candidates). Bug B2: the Excalidraw build script threw on the first missing referenced image; added a pre-flight in both identical copies (skill-packs/ and scripts/diagrams/) that collects every missing or dangling asset (including a directory or broken symlink masquerading as a file) and fails once with the full list before ever writing a board with dangling embeds. 31 regression tests added, security-reviewed clean.
summary: Fix co-located Excalidraw board folders splitting in the knowledge tree, blank embedded images, and dangling-embed builds

description: Add .github/workflows/ci.yml running npm test on every push to main and every PR, Node 20 runner, npm ci install — closing the gap that let the capture-guard q030 recall regression ship unnoticed at v0.10.1/v0.10.2 (only desktop-release.yml existed before, and it never ran vitest). Separately bumped all four GitHub Actions pinned in desktop-release.yml off their Node-20-runtime majors onto Node-24-native versions (actions/checkout@v4->v7, actions/setup-node@v4->v6, actions/cache@v4->v6, softprops/action-gh-release@v2->v3) ahead of GitHub's Node 20 deprecation; each bump verified to declare using: node24 before applying. The setup-node build-target node-version:'20' is unrelated (build Node, not action runtime) and was left as-is.
summary: Add the project's first CI workflow (vitest on push/PR to main) and bump desktop-release.yml actions off Node 20

description: A 200-capture stress test displaced gold query q030 (a Turkish query with zero raw-BM25 overlap with its English target, resting entirely on derived signals) below a flood of synthetic capture entries. Root cause was genuine crowding, not test flakiness, so the fix tunes CAPTURE_RANK_PENALTY down from 0.5 to 0.4 in src/lib/recall.ts rather than loosening the assertion — a displacement sweep showed the guard holds at every penalty <=0.45, with 0.4 sitting below the cliff (~16% margin) while a strongly-matching capture can still surface. The change only scales down the derived rankScore for capture docs (digest#/bookmark#), never the raw hit.score the memory-recall hook gates on, so it is monotonically safe: measured recall@3 degradation from capture flood dropped 3.3pts to 0.0pts.
summary: Recall: tune CAPTURE_RANK_PENALTY 0.5→0.4 to stop capture-flood from displacing a genuine top-3 result (q030)

description: Root-cause fix for dashboard-server processes leaking (~25 observed) after repeated vault window open/close: the Tauri shell only killed the server on RunEvent::ExitRequested, which never fires on force-quit, crash, tauri dev Ctrl+C, or a dev-rebuild, so the server got reparented to launchd and lived forever holding its loopback port. New src/server/lifecycle.ts startParentDeathWatch() polls the recorded parent PID (via kill(pid,0), ESRCH=gone) and self-exits the server on any parent-death path; trackChild()/killTrackedChildren() reaps the server's own PTY children (node-pty claude sessions setsid into their own session so the Rust process-group kill can't reach them). Rust side hardened as belt-and-suspenders: spawns the child in its own process group and reap_server() sends SIGTERM then SIGKILL after 1.5s to the whole group on ExitRequested/Exit, replacing the old uncatchable SIGKILL that prevented the server from ever running its own cleanup. Gated on DREAMCONTEXT_DESKTOP=1 so a terminal-run dashboard stays user-managed.
summary: Fix orphaned dashboard-server processes: parent-death watchdog + PTY reaper + graceful process-group SIGTERM from the Tauri shell

description: Add a local-image-to-hosted-asset bridge for GitHub task sync. Push uploads embedded local images (agent-drop screenshots) via the Contents API to a dedicated dreamcontext-assets branch (created off default-branch HEAD, never touching the working tree), rewriting each markdown reference to its hosted /raw/ URL wire-only so the local mirror keeps its canonical path. Pull maps the hosted URL back to the local path before the 3-way merge so images never churn. Paths are content-addressed so identical bytes dedupe and re-pushes are idempotent (422-on-existing swallowed); non-image references are sniffed-and-skipped, https/data refs left untouched. New shared src/lib/image-sniff.ts and src/lib/task-backend/github-assets.ts codec. An adversarial review round caught a Critical: resolveLocalImagePath honored arbitrary absolute paths and buffered the file before the size cap; fixed with an allow-listed root-containment guard (isInsideRoot) and a resolveImageBytes choke point that stat-gates size before read. 14+3 new tests, github suite green.
summary: GitHub task sync: local task images upload to a dreamcontext-assets branch and render inline on the synced issue

description: Fix a drag-cursor-stuck regression in the Timeline Gantt and ship accompanying visual polish. Root cause: window pointermove and pointerup listeners were registered inside the drag-start handler and removed by a cleanup effect that re-ran on every render. The first setState from a pointermove call triggered a React re-render; the cleanup effect fired and removed the listeners mid-drag; pointerup was never received, leaving the cursor permanently in the grabbing state. Fix: hold the updateTask mutation callback in a ref (updateTaskRef) and write a stable endDrag closure that reads the ref; gate the listener-teardown cleanup to component unmount only (empty dependency array). Drag commits now fire reliably and the cursor resets to auto on release. Visual polish shipped in the same commit: row height raised from 34 to 48 px, bar labels use larger type, zoom ladder rescaled to 10–100 px/day, a ResizeObserver stretches days to fill the scroll viewport on wide screens, and the label column widens from 220 to 240 px.
summary: Fix Timeline Gantt grabbing-cursor stuck mid-drag (ref-stabilize callbacks); polish: taller bars (34→48px), viewport-fill zoom, wider label column

description: Add multi-assignee rendering to the board task card and list row. BoardCard.tsx previously called the singular taskAssignee() — returning only the first person: tag — so tasks with multiple co-assignees showed a single badge. Switch to taskAssignees() and render an AvatarStack: up to 3 overlapping initials badges plus a +N overflow chip whose tooltip lists the remaining names; per-person name tooltips on each badge. Add assigneeHue(slug) to boardModel.ts — a deterministic per-person hue using the same modular-arithmetic idiom as tagHue and council modelHue — so co-assignees are colour-distinguishable at a glance. The avatar ring uses --color-bg-elevated for clean overlap gaps. Work is uncommitted (session 573e0ec4).
summary: Add AvatarStack to Kanban/list card: all person: assignees rendered (up to 3 + +N overflow), deterministic per-person hue via assigneeHue()

description: Two new desktop-gated, loopback-only server routes support the multi-session agent surface. POST /api/agent/drop accepts image drops from within the agent terminal, validates via magic-byte detection and per-chunk size cap, writes the image to a vault temp file, and injects the file path into the active PTY. GET /api/agent/sessions returns the per-vault session roster (session ids, start times, status), used by the surface to rebuild the tab list after navigation or app restart. The roster file (state/.agent-sessions.json) is gitignored as machine-local. Accompanying unit test suites added for both routes (agent-drop.test.ts, agent-sessions.test.ts).
summary: Add /api/agent/drop (image-drop vault injection) and /api/agent/sessions (per-vault roster) server routes

description: Add a ⌘K command palette (CommandPalette.tsx + CommandPalette.css) that queries /api/recall with live BM25 results and an optional 'intelligent' Haiku toggle for intent-aware ranking. Each hit shows type, title, and a description excerpt. Clicking a result navigates the app to the correct page AND opens the document directly: a recallNav.ts lookup maps every corpus type (knowledge, feature, task, data-structure, changelog) to the destination page, and a useFocusTarget.ts hook threads focus signals from the top-level PageRouter down into each page component so clicking a knowledge hit both navigates to KnowledgePage and expands the matching doc. Focus wiring propagated through FeaturesPage, KnowledgePage, CorePage, and TasksPage. Palette opens on ⌘K, closes on Escape or outside click, and is positioned as a centered sheet over the app content.
summary: Add ⌘K command palette: live BM25 + Haiku intelligent toggle; clicking a hit navigates to and opens the doc

description: claude --resume <id> returned 'No conversation found' for any session tab that had never been used (no transcript yet at ~/.claude/projects/*/<id>.jsonl). The server-side agent spawn now globs for the transcript file before choosing the resume strategy: when the jsonl exists, run claude --resume <id>; when it does not, start a fresh session pinned to the same id via claude --session-id <id>. The fresh session becomes resumable once the user sends a first message. This prevents a tab that was opened but never typed in from erroring on its first explicit start.
summary: Fix auto-resume when no transcript exists: check jsonl file presence before --resume; fall back to --session-id

description: Root-cause fix for drag-to-split failing in the Tauri WKWebView. Two platform constraints required the workaround: (1) WKWebView does NOT deliver the HTML5 drop event to drop targets that are conditionally rendered (mounted mid-drag), even though dragover fires on them fine. (2) WKWebView strips custom-MIME dataTransfer.getData() values on the drop event — only text/plain survives. The fix: store the dragged session id in a React ref (set on dragstart, cleared on dragend); run the split/combine action on the reliable dragend event of the source element instead of on drop. Note: board drag-and-drop works only because it uses text/plain and always-mounted targets — neither property holds for the dynamically-mounted pane split zones. This pattern must be followed for any future drag-to-action that targets conditionally-rendered elements in WKWebView.
summary: Fix drag-to-split on WKWebView: carry session id in ref; action executes on dragend not drop

description: Extend the agent surface to support multiple simultaneous Claude sessions side-by-side. Per-pane tab bars sit directly above the terminal they drive, providing clear tab↔pane correspondence. ⌘D splits into side-by-side panes; ⌘T opens a new tab; ⌘W closes the active tab. A session can be minimized out of the panes into a live-progress chip in a corner dock floating above the overlay (sibling of .agent-surface, not a child — see the contain:layout-paint gotcha in 2.memory.md). Clicking the chip restores the session as a pane. Active-pane accent makes the focused pane visually prominent; clicking any terminal anywhere activates its pane. The overlay auto-collapses on sidebar or palette navigation (via a dreamcontext-navigate window event). Crisp bordered SVG icon buttons for minimize and close; the per-tab restart button and its confirm/ConfirmDialog code were removed. PTY/xterm/WebSocket instances survive tab-switch, pane-move, collapse/expand, and navigation via a detached-DOM garage.
summary: Add multi-session split-pane agent surface: per-pane tab bars, minimize-to-corner dock, active-pane accent, ⌘D/T/W shortcuts

description: Replace the single-file AgentSurface.tsx (1166 lines) with a five-module split: AgentSurface, AgentTabs, AgentFab, AgentDock, PaneFragment. Add a top-bar bypass button and + (new tab) shortcut via React portal so controls sit outside the scrollable terminal region. Introduce the four-state dock mascot (sleeping/thinking/working/waiting) with per-state animated eyes, aura gradient, caption chip, and mood-color tokens. Enlarge pane-header buttons and add a11y + hit-target fixes throughout (restart-orphan guard, idle-timer-on-close, dock-close a11y, slot mount guards). Shipped as the foundation pass for the multi-session surface.
summary: Redesign agent surface: four-state mascot dock, top-bar bypass/+ controls, 5-module split of AgentSurface.tsx (1166→477 lines)

description: Fix the dashboard static server returning index.html (Content-Type: text/html) for any path missing on disk, including hashed JS/CSS chunks. Under the continuous-app-update model, a stale tab or WKWebView referenced previous-build asset hashes that no longer exist; the SPA fallback returned HTML under a script type=module request, intermittently tripping the 'text/html is not a valid JavaScript MIME type' error. Fix gates the SPA fallback to genuine navigation requests (Accept: text/html header or extensionless path); a request for a missing file that carries a file extension now returns a proper 404. Adds .mjs/.map/.webp to the MIME type table as defensive hardening (.mjs would otherwise serve as application/octet-stream — the same bug class). New tests/unit/server-static.test.ts reproduces the reported failure.
summary: Gate SPA fallback to navigation requests; missing asset files with extensions now return 404; add .mjs/.map/.webp to MIME table

description: Map three macOS editing gestures in the embedded xterm to the control sequences Claude Code's interactive prompt already honors, using attachCustomKeyEventHandler (xterm does not emit these combos itself). Shift+Enter sends a backslash+CR continuation for a newline without submitting; Option+Delete sends Ctrl+W (\x17) to delete the previous word; Cmd+Delete sends Ctrl+U (\x15) to delete to the line start. All other keys fall through to xterm unchanged.
summary: Add macOS line-editing keybindings to the in-app Agent terminal: Shift+Enter (newline), Option+Delete (delete word), Cmd+Delete (delete to line start)

description: The board model read only the legacy scalar assignee field for filtering, grouping, and card display — tasks assigned via person:<slug> tags (which the detail panel writes) showed as Unassigned everywhere except the detail panel. Fix introduces taskAssignees() that derives all assignees from person: tags with a scalar fallback, and taskAssignee() for the primary. Filter matching is now multi-aware (matchAssignee checks all assignees); group-by places a task under each assignee bucket it belongs to; the KanbanBoard assignee option list is built from every person referenced across tasks; BoardToolbar per-assignee counts are multi-aware; TimelineGantt row label uses the derived primary assignee.
summary: Board reads assignees from person:<slug> tags in filter, group-by, card properties, and Gantt; legacy scalar field retained as fallback

description: Add mutual exclusion to the sleep consolidation path. sleep_started_at in .sleep.json is the primary lock; inspectSleepLock() centralizes read + 30m stale TTL so a crashed consolidation self-heals instead of wedging the brain permanently. sleep start refuses a live lock (exit 1), auto-reclaims a stale one, and accepts --force for deliberate override. Hook directives/reminders suppress under a live lock but fall through a stale one. The Tauri launcher rejects a concurrent desktop Sleep with a 409 response. An atomic O_EXCL file stamp lock (src/lib/file-lock.ts, 60s stale TTL) closes the cross-process check-then-stamp race: 8/16-way parallel sleep start now yields exactly 1 winner (was 2-4 before). Separately, the debt scale was rescaled ×2: Alert 0-7 / Drowsy 8-13 / Sleepy 14-19 / Must Sleep 20+; directives fire at >=8 / >=14 / >=20; rhythm reminder at 5 sessions. Thresholds centralized as named constants (DEBT_DROWSY/DEBT_SLEEPY/DEBT_MUST_SLEEP/RHYTHM_SESSIONS) in sleep-consolidation.ts as the single source of truth — PRD, SKILL.md, and system-flow docs updated to match.
summary: Atomic consolidation mutex + debt scale rescaled ×2: sleep_started_at is the lock; O_EXCL stamp lock (file-lock.ts) makes check-then-stamp race-free; 30m stale TTL self-heals crashes; Alert 0-7/Drowsy 8-13/Sleepy 14-19/Must Sleep 20+

description: Add in-app prerequisite detection and installation to the Agent screen. GET /api/agent/capabilities now additionally reports claudeCli, nodePty, and npm availability, each probed via $SHELL -ilc so the detection path matches the PTY spawn's PATH (finds claude in ~/.local/bin, etc.). New POST /api/agent/install {claude|pty} and GET /api/agent/install/status endpoints mirror the Sleepy capture-run Map+poll pattern: the claude install target runs npm i -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code; the pty target installs node-pty into the CLI package directory, then chmods the spawn-helper and busts the probe cache. A Setup panel on the Agent screen renders any missing prerequisite with a one-click install button and re-checks on success. Start-agent is now gated on both node-pty and claude being present. All install endpoints are desktop-gated, loopback-only, and use a closed target whitelist.
summary: In-app prerequisite installer: capabilities endpoint reports claudeCli/nodePty/npm; one-click install panel; Start-agent gated on both

description: Fix unreadable Claude Code output in the embedded in-app Agent terminal on both light and dark themes. readXtermTheme() in AgentSurface.tsx was mapping ANSI black/white/brightBlack directly to design tokens, which inverted luminance in light mode and made dim grays too light in dark mode; minimumContrastRatio was set to 1, disabling xterm's contrast safety-net. Claude's TUI blocks (ANSI 7/8 background + default foreground) collapsed to same-luminance-on-same-luminance and were unreadable. Fix: set minimumContrastRatio to 4.5 and replaced the design-token mapping with a conventional per-theme grayscale ANSI ramp so ANSI dark/light colors follow each theme's luminance expectations correctly.
summary: Fix Agent terminal unreadable text in both themes (minimumContrastRatio 4.5 + conventional ANSI grayscale ramp)

description: Add three virtual 'smart' bucket rows to the Tasks board Version filter (BoardToolbar.tsx), rendered above the literal sprint list with the same is/not toggle and live task counts: Current (the active planning sprint, gated on .active-version.json), Backlog (backlog-tagged tasks, folds out the literal 'backlog' row to avoid duplication), and Completed (any task on a released sprint, collapsing the full released-version set into one selection). Implemented via @current/@backlog/@completed pseudo-tokens that never collide with real version names. versionTokenMatches/matchVersionField in boardModel.ts resolve tokens against a VersionMeta object (active version string + released version set) at filter time; filterTasks() accepts an optional versionMeta (backward compatible, defaults to EMPTY_VERSION_META). KanbanBoard.tsx wires useActiveVersion + released-versions list into versionMeta and threads it into both filterTasks calls and the toolbar. normVersionFilter() in boardModel.ts migrates any pre-existing literal 'backlog' token in a saved view's inc/exc array to @backlog on load (idempotent, case-insensitive) so old saved views are not orphaned as un-dismissable ghosts. Committed c8a910d on feat/board-version-smart-buckets, merged via PR #104.
summary: Board version filter gains Current/Backlog/Completed smart buckets — @-prefixed virtual tokens resolve against live active/released state at filter time

description: Released v0.10.0 to both distribution channels. npm publish of 0.10.0 is live on the registry. The GitHub desktop-release CI workflow (macos-14, .github/workflows/desktop-release.yml) built and published the v0.10.0 GitHub Release with the ad-hoc deep-signed dreamcontext-beta_0.10.0_aarch64.app.tar.gz (29.3 MB) and accompanying .sha256 artifact. The workflow derives the app version from the git tag (VERSION=tag minus v prefix), so tauri.conf.json must stay in sync for the internal .app version to match. RELEASES.json v0.10.0 status set to released, date 2026-06-29. v0.11.0 opened as the new active planning milestone (desktop stale-dist fix, task-derived roadmap, excalidraw card-overflow fix, web read-only public surface).
summary: v0.10.0 released: npm publish live + GitHub desktop-release CI published v0.10.0 .app

description: Rebuilt AgentSurface.tsx and AgentTerminal.css for native macOS quality UX. User pain points fixed first: (1) Close/restart now works on first click by consuming the mousedown event before the blur handler fires, preventing the click-twice-to-close issue. (2) A native-style confirmation sheet appears before ending a still-running Claude session (Enter to confirm, Esc to cancel); already-ended sessions close instantly without confirmation. Additional UX polish: always-visible active-tab close button (previously only appeared on hover, so it was undiscoverable); middle-click closes a tab; bigger hit targets on all interactive controls; an ended-session pane shows a prominent Reconnect button instead of a tiny hover icon; persistent split-pane headers show the pane label at all times so users know which pane they are acting on; double-click renames a tab inline; status dots in the tab strip (green/amber/red) encode live/idle/ended state at a glance. A11y: added keyboard-navigable confirm dialog; reduced-motion media query skips animations.
summary: Rebuild Agent section UX: native confirmation sheet, bigger hit targets, middle-click close, status dots, reconnect overlay

description: Fixed find_global_cli in desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs to use an interactive login shell (-ilc) instead of the prior non-interactive form. A non-interactive shell skips ~/.zshrc, making nvm-managed binaries (including the dreamcontext CLI itself) invisible to the Finder-launched Tauri app. The -ilc flag forces rc-file sourcing so the full user PATH is available at app launch time. Also bumped desktop/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json from 0.9.2 to 0.10.0, which was stale; the built .app now reports the correct version in About and Sparkle update checks. Both fixes shipped in the v0.10.0 desktop release: the GitHub CI workflow (desktop-release.yml, macos-14 runner) built, deep-signed, and published dreamcontext-beta_0.10.0_aarch64.app.tar.gz (29.3 MB) + .sha256 to the GitHub Release.
summary: find_global_cli uses -ilc so nvm-installed CLI is visible; tauri.conf.json bumped to 0.10.0

description: Add rename and delete controls to every row of the Versions popover in the dashboard (VersionsPopover.tsx). PATCH /api/releases/:version was extended to support rename: it rewrites the RELEASES.json entry, re-points every task whose version field held the old string, moves the active-planning pointer if needed, returns 409 on a name collision, and supports unregistered ghost versions (derived from tasks with no RELEASES.json entry) with no-op on the absent entry. A new DELETE /api/releases/:version implements warn-and-clear semantics: the entry is dropped, any task referencing it has its version field cleared (task body is preserved), and the active-planning pointer is also cleared. A shared repointTasksVersion() helper in changelog.ts centralises the re-pointing logic across rename and delete. New React hooks useRenameVersion and useDeleteVersion wire the backend into the popover. Nine focused unit tests in tests/unit/releases-rename-delete-route.test.ts cover rename/ghost/collision/active-move and delete/ghost/active-clear/404 cases. Ghost-cleanup session also re-pointed two 0.9.2 typo tasks to the real v0.9.2 and cleared the v0.1.0 ghost.
summary: In-app version rename and delete: popover controls + PATCH-rename/DELETE routes with task re-pointing

description: Five fixes to the dashboard shipped in this cycle. (1) Board overflow: added min-width:0 to .shell-main (Shell.css) so the flex container shrinks below its content min-width; without this the nowrap toolbar (~1100px) made the entire board overflow the window, clipping the first Kanban column under the sidebar. (2) Responsive toolbar: added a ResizeObserver-driven 'More' overflow chip to BoardToolbar — View/Group/Versions/Properties collapse into it when scrollWidth > clientWidth; their existing popover bodies render as left-flyouts from the More panel; the search box still shrinks fully before anything collapses. (3) WKWebView drag-highlight flicker: removed per-column onColumnDragLeave from KanbanBoard (which fires on silhouette reflow and on WKWebView's null relatedTarget mid-drag), and instead clears dragOverKey at the board-row container level only when relatedTarget is non-null and genuinely outside the row; drop/dragEnd still call endDrag() for reliable cleanup. (4) Knowledge subfolder slug 404: BrainSearch now derives the knowledge slug from hit.path (knowledge/.../x.md -> .../x) instead of the bare hit.slug field, so sub-folder knowledge files open correctly. (5) Settings page max-width corrected from hardcoded 1080px to the --page-max-width token (1200px), matching all other pages.
summary: Fix Tasks board overflow (Shell.css min-width:0), responsive BoardToolbar 'More' menu, WKWebView drag flicker, knowledge subfolder slug 404, settings width token

description: Four interrelated task-management improvements to the dashboard. (1) Eisenhower matrix drag-drop: tasks can now be dragged between the four urgency/importance quadrants directly on the matrix view. (2) Sprint-aware version filter: the Tasks board Versions popover highlights the active planning sprint and filters tasks to it inline. (3) In-review status color changed to red (--color-error token), applying consistently to the Kanban column border/dot, filter chips, RICE/calendar status indicators, and the new context menu. (4) Context menu flyout submenus in the Kanban card right-click menu: 'Move to status' and 'Set priority' now open compact flyout submenus adjacent to the parent row (with viewport-edge flip-left guard) instead of expanding all statuses/priorities inline, reducing the menu's vertical footprint from ~360px to ~190px. Escape-to-close, current-value check marks, and all selection callbacks preserved.
summary: Add Eisenhower matrix drag-drop between quadrants; sprint-aware version filter; in-review status red; context menu flyout submenus

description: Removed the h1.page-title headline from every page that still had one: Core Files, Settings, Sleep, Packs, Taxonomy, and the Council header (title + LAB badge + tagline). The sidebar nav already labels the active page, making per-page headlines redundant chrome. 'New debate' moved from the Council header into the search/filter toolbar row as the primary action, anchored right. Core Files page now derives an active file as selected ?? first-file on load, matching the Knowledge/Tasks/Features master-detail pattern — no more empty 'Select a file to view.' state on first landing. The change applies uniformly: if there is a sidebar label, there is no page headline.
summary: Remove page headlines from all dashboard pages; Core Files shows first file by default instead of empty state

description: Rebuilt the Council page with two distinct states. Empty state: a brand showcase explainer with a 'Put your hardest decisions to a council.' headline, an animated FlowDiagram stage showing 6 persona archetypes (Architect, Pragmatist, Skeptic, Researcher, Advocate, Strategist) debating in a ring and converging into a synthesizer verdict, plus a 'Start your first debate' CTA. Populated state: Council + LAB badge header, a dismissible 'How Council works' banner carrying the mini animated diagram above the search bar, and the existing debate grid below. The sidebar Council entry now carries a LAB chip. 'New debate' moved out of the header and into the filter toolbar row. Additionally added ?page=<page> deep-link support in Shell.tsx so loading the dashboard with a ?page= param lands directly on that page, skipping the remembered nav state.
summary: Redesign Council page: animated empty-state showcase, LAB badge, dismissible how-it-works banner, and ?page= deep-link support

description: Rebuilt the Sleep page into a single continuous card. getSleepMood(debt) drives the hero's Sleepy face: awake (debt <=6), drowsy (7-9), asleep (>=10) with floating Zzz animation, all inside a level-tinted aura. The separate Sleep cloud panel was folded into the same card below a hairline border-top divider with a faint violet wash at the seam. The redundant 'Sleep cloud' header, cloud glyph, and subtitle were removed; the queued-item count moved up into the hero headline as a small accent pill (visible only when there is something queued). The card chrome is now a single .sleep-card element tinted by debt level via modifier classes (--alert / --drowsy / --sleepy / --must_sleep). CSS dead-weight for the old separate card (puffed top, shadow, own heading) removed.
summary: Redesign Sleep page: mood-driven Sleepy mascot (debt-aware face) and sleep-cloud merged into one continuous card

description: Upgraded the embedded Claude Code terminal (AgentTerminal.tsx) to production grade across four dimensions. (1) Render quality: added @xterm/addon-webgl for GPU-rendered crisp text matching Zed crispness, with automatic canvas-renderer fallback on WebGL context-loss. Sometype Mono font is pre-loaded and re-applied one frame after open so the WebGL glyph atlas builds with correct cell widths, fixing stretched glyphs. (2) Session persistence: hoisted the terminal instance (xterm, WebSocket, PTY state) above App.tsx's switch(nav.page) so navigating to Tasks, Council, or any page and back keeps the same live Claude Code session alive; only CSS visibility toggles, nothing unmounts on page navigation. (3) Drag-to-split panes: dragging one Agent tab onto another merges them into a side-by-side terminal split; the divider is draggable to resize. (4) Removed the bypass-warning banner from the terminal header; the 'bypass' pill in the tab strip remains. Also added a BETA badge to the Agent tab consistent with Council LAB / Settings BETA styling.
summary: Add production-grade Agent terminal: WebGL renderer, session persistence above App.tsx switch, BETA badge, Sometype Mono fix, drag-to-split

description: Full rebuild of the dashboard Tasks board (Board.dc.html design reference) into a production violet-themed surface. New capabilities: (1) Saved views — each view carries its own independently persisted filter, sort, and grouping configuration; BoardViewTabs.tsx renders the tab rail; views are named and managed via the toolbar. (2) Two-pane include/exclude Filter menu — a FilterPopover with separate include/exclude panels for status, priority, tags, version, and assignee. (3) Versions popover — filter to one or more planning versions directly from the toolbar. (4) Card Properties — toggleable property badges on BoardCard.tsx (due date, RICE score, assignees). (5) AtRiskAlert.tsx surfaces tasks that are past due or blocked. New files: dashboard/src/components/tasks/{boardModel.ts, useBoard.ts, BoardViewTabs.tsx, BoardToolbar.tsx, BoardColumn.tsx, BoardCard.tsx, SaveScopeDialog.tsx, AtRiskAlert.tsx, Board.css}. Persistence model: board preferences (views, sorts, filters, grouping) split across a version-controlled overrides/board.json ('save for all' — permanent and team-shareable, never lost to a browser origin or per-launch loopback port) and a gitignored state/board.local.json ('save for yourself' — per-machine overrides and private views), merged on read by the client. SaveScopeDialog presents the user with the choice on every save action. New backend routes GET /api/board, PUT /api/board/shared, PUT /api/board/local (src/server/routes/board.ts, mirrors the ui-settings.ts pattern). Existing Eisenhower, Timeline, Calendar, Heatmap, and RICE views plus the rich task detail panel are preserved.
summary: Rebuild Tasks board to violet design: saved views, two-pane filter/sort/group, Versions popover, card Properties, shared+local persistence

description: Three related fixes to the transcript distill and salience pipeline (src/cli/commands/transcript.ts, src/lib/salience.ts, src/lib/reflection.ts). (1) transcript.ts:131-140: user-role blocks containing tool_result content were being extracted into userMessages — these are pure tool output, not user text, and were causing sub-agent task-notification XML and skill-loader headers to be misidentified as user messages and scored for salience. Fix: the tool_result branch is removed; only type:text blocks in user-role messages are collected. (2) CORRECTION_RE in salience.ts was matching bare 'no' and 'değil' (Turkish negative) anywhere in a message, generating false correction bookmarks on any sentence containing those words. Narrowed the regex to genuine correction phrases ('no, that's wrong', 'incorrect', 'hayır doğru değil', etc.). (3) REFLECTION_NOISE in reflection.ts extended with sub-agent chrome tokens (toolu, agentid, subagent, notification, success, resum) so these never surface as reflect candidates. Coverage tests added for all three. 66 affected-file tests pass post-fix.
summary: Fix bookmark/salience pipeline: drop tool_result from userMessages, narrow CORRECTION_RE, add REFLECTION_NOISE for sub-agent chrome

description: Three related Sleepy notch / desktop window fixes shipped across multiple sessions. (1) Enabled opt-in: a new PanelEnabled atomic bool in Rust (desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs) defaults to false — the notch perch and hover-open watcher no longer start unconditionally at launch, so users who have never turned Sleepy on no longer see the notch appear. A new `sleepy:enabled` JS event (sent by applySleepyHotkey in dashboard/src/lib/sleepy.ts, which runs on mount, on storage sync, and on every Settings change) mirrors the persisted enabled flag into Rust's apply_sleepy_enabled, which shows or hides the perch and closes any open panel accordingly. (2) Perch sizing: perch width reduced from 104 px to 56 px and right padding from 8 px to 3 px so the tongue fits tightly to the notch face without overflow. (3) Titlebar text-selection: added user-select:none and -webkit-user-select:none to the .header rule in the launcher CSS; previously a double-click on the vault name in the title bar selected the text rather than triggering window maximize/restore, breaking the expected Tauri double-click behavior.
summary: Fix Sleepy notch: opt-in by default, perch tight-to-notch, titlebar user-select prevents double-click text selection

description: Add src/server/routes/sleepy-chat.ts and useSleepyChat.ts hook. The Sleepy Ask panel now supports real, multi-turn Claude Code conversations running inside the active vault's project directory, streamed over SSE. A first message spawns `claude -p "<msg>" --output-format stream-json`; subsequent messages use `--resume <sessionId>` for continuity; reset drops the stored session. Read-only is enforced three ways: (1) `--permission-mode plan` (load-bearing: blocks all mutating/action tools including Write/Edit/Bash and any connected MCP write tools without enumeration); (2) `--disallowedTools` removes orchestration tools (Task, Skill, Agent, CronCreate, etc.) so the chat cannot fan out into sub-agents or background flows; (3) a guard system prompt tells Sleepy to ignore session directives and just answer. Two tiers exposed in the UI as 'normal' (sonnet) and 'intelligent' (opus); the real model names are hidden from the UI. Session id + transcript persist server-side in the vault's state directory, surviving the desktop app's per-launch loopback-port change that would otherwise wipe localStorage.
summary: Add Sleepy chat: real Claude Code SSE chat via `claude -p --resume` with read-only enforcement and normal/intelligent model tiers

description: Two related Sleepy page fixes shipped together. First: the type-constellation and the filter chips were driven by two separate state variables — constSel (single-select, drives constellation highlight) and types[] (multi-select, drives chips) — that could drift apart whenever either was touched. Removed constSel entirely; types[] is now the single source of truth for both UIs. selectConst() delegates to toggleType(); the constellation's selected/dim classes derive from types.includes(t); the center gem and 'All' chip both clear types[]. Constellation is now multi-select (consistent with chips). Second: the Ask tab's composeAnswer call had no error handling on the /api/recall/haiku fetch — a server timeout, 500, or network drop left the answer pane blank. The catch now falls back to recallOnce (direct BM25 search) and flips the answer badge to 'local recall · grounded' so Ask always returns something.
summary: Fix Sleepy page: unify constellation/chip state; add Haiku fallback to BM25 on server error

description: The Sleepy nav rail item now uses a SleepyEyes sub-component — two eye spans animated with a CSS scaleY blink every 4.4s, matching the blink timing of the idle mascot face on the Ask/Search page. The eye marks are the same two-eyes avatar that fronts Sleepy's answer bubbles, scaled down to sit inside the shared .sidebar-icon container so it reads as one of the family.
summary: Add blinking Sleepy eyes animation to sidebar nav icon (scaleY blink every 4.4s, matching Ask bubble mascot)

description: Replaced the flat 9-item sidebar list with a hierarchical, job-to-be-done structure. Four groups: Workspace (Sleepy, Tasks, Council — surfaces you actively drive each day), Memory (Core, Knowledge, Features, Taxonomy — the brain's stored content), Brain (Map, Sleep Cycle — graph health and consolidation), Control Panel (Packs, Settings — unchanged). Key naming decisions: 'Sleepy' stays as the first Workspace item (it is the ask/recall surface, not a notch config); the consolidation page is renamed 'Sleep Cycle' (not 'Sleep') to avoid the Sleepy/Sleep naming collision; the 3D graph page is renamed 'Map' (not 'Brain') to prevent a Brain-in-Brain label. Old names retired: 'Core Files' → 'Core', 'Brain' → 'Map', 'Sleep State' → 'Sleep Cycle'. Sidebar.tsx, I18nContext.tsx updated; new SleepyEyes SVG sub-component added for the Sleepy rail icon.
summary: Restructure sidebar nav into 4 job-based groups (Workspace / Memory / Brain / Control Panel)

description: Add `dreamcontext taxonomy audit --fix` (commit bd45856). Before this command existed, fixing non-canonical tags required per-task manual edits. `audit --fix` runs a single in-process pass: reads taxonomy.json vocabulary, scans every task file under state/ and knowledge files for any tag that maps to a canonical faceted alias (e.g. 'agent' → 'topic:agents', 'cli' → 'topic:cli'), and rewrites the files in bulk without touching other frontmatter. Normalized ~89 tags across active tasks and knowledge in the post-deep-research consolidation cycle. The dry-run path (audit without --fix) is unchanged — it still only reports drift.
summary: Add `taxonomy audit --fix` to bulk-normalize non-canonical tags to canonical faceted form in one shot

description: Add dashboard/src/pages/SleepyPage.tsx (+.css) implementing the design's Search.dc.html. Search tab delivers instant local BM25 over the full brain corpus (knowledge, features, tasks, memory, changelog); Ask tab composes a grounded, extractive answer purely from top recall hits — no LLM, zero tokens, inline citations — upholding the 'local · instant · no tokens' promise. Results display in flat or grouped mode with a context-constellation idle state; selecting a hit opens a side-panel rendered by the canonical per-type renderer (DocContent.tsx fetches the full record via /api/knowledge|features|tasks/:slug because recall stores only extracted board text for Excalidraw items). New dashboard/src/hooks/useRecall.ts drives the debounced search. New src/server/routes/recall.ts (registered in src/server/index.ts) exposes GET /api/recall?q=&types=&top= over bm25Search/buildCorpus with an 8-second corpus cache — the first HTTP surface of recall to the dashboard. Sleepy is registered as the first Workspace nav item and the default landing page (App.tsx, Shell.tsx, Sidebar.tsx, I18nContext.tsx).
summary: Add 'Sleepy' Search/Ask surface: instant BM25 local search + extractive zero-token Ask composed from recall hits

description: Add dreamcontext-deep-research, a core orchestration skill that is the heavy iterative counterpart to dreamcontext-explore for questions that need synthesis across large or federated corpora. Where explore is a single-pass Haiku agent with a tight budget, deep-research decomposes the question, fans out parallel explore searchers over the full curated corpus and connected peer vaults, gap-loops until coverage is sufficient, adversarially verifies load-bearing claims, then synthesizes a cited report. Crucially, it reuses dreamcontext-explore as its searcher and verifier — no new sub-agent, no new codex mirror to maintain — a deliberate design choice enforced by the Claude Code constraint that sub-agents cannot reliably fan out to further sub-agents. Named dreamcontext-deep-research (not the generic deep-research) to avoid colliding with the web-search skill. Installed as a core artifact via installCoreForPlatform, mirroring the curator and initializer blocks, so dreamcontext update refreshes it into existing projects. Discoverability wired via an escalation note in agents/dreamcontext-explore.md, the Sub-Agents section of skill/SKILL.md, and a two-depths-of-search note in skill/references/knowledge-and-recall.md. Decision rationale captured in knowledge/deep-research-mode-decision.md. 2307 tests pass, tsc clean.
summary: Add `dreamcontext-deep-research` core skill: corpus-native iterative synthesizer that fans out `dreamcontext-explore` searchers over full vault + peers and returns cited reports

description: Fix for GitHub issue #74. The recall-capture-stress test suite had hardcoded 30s per-assertion timeouts that regularly exceeded on a machine under parallel load (observed 105-126s wall-clock vs ~50s baseline on a quiet machine). Two inline timeouts raised to 90s — sufficient headroom for a 2-2.5x overloaded machine at the issue's stated ~50s baseline. Test logic and assertions are unchanged; this is a pure timeout-threshold adjustment. PR #83 (fix/74-recall-stress-timeout) is open against main, not yet merged.
summary: Raise recall-capture-stress inline timeouts 30s->90s to prevent false flake on loaded machines

description: Two related fixes shipped together on PR #79 (fix/77-rename-sync-dcid, closes #77 and #78). First: remote sync reconciliation was keyed on task name-slug, which is mutable — renaming a task caused it to be treated as a new remote item rather than an update to the existing one. Fixed by keying reconciliation on the stable dcId field instead. Second: the delta-pull watermark-gating meant that assignees set below the watermark on the remote side were never re-examined, leaving pre-existing drift (e.g. 9 remotely-assigned tasks with no local person: tag) unhealed indefinitely. Fix adds detectAssigneeDrift() (read-only) + reconcileAssignees() (idempotent heal that re-baselines the ledger and journals the change) on both ClickUp and GitHub backends; merge.ts gains planAssigneeHeal() which heals only when remote moved and local did not, so no pending-push local assignment is ever clobbered. New CLI surface: tasks sync --reconcile (heal) and tasks doctor --remote (surface drift without modifying). 10 new tests covering gap reproduction, idempotent heal, and pending-assignment non-clobber. PR is open against main, not yet merged.
summary: Key remote sync reconciliation on stable dcId (not name-slug); add sync --reconcile to heal pre-existing assignee drift

description: Add ask:boolean to CustomFieldDef (overrides.ts parse + upsert + server POST /task-overrides/fields + useTasks.ts type). When ask:true, renderOverrideBriefing tags the field [ASK THE USER] and emits an ASK-FIRST rule: the agent must ask the user for the value before creating the task, using the field's prompt as the question, and wait for the answer before proceeding. This captures human-judgment fields (e.g. time estimates, business-impact calls) that the agent should not infer on its own. In autonomous contexts with no user to ask (sleep reconcile, headless runs), the agent leaves the field unset and notes it — never invents a value. Dashboard: AddCustomFieldForm gains an 'Ask me' toggle alongside the Required toggle. Tests: 3 new cases in overrides.test.ts (ask parse, ask tag in briefing, ask-without-prompt). All 161 unit test files green; CLI + dashboard typecheck clean.
summary: Custom field ask flag: agent asks user interactively instead of inferring; ASK-FIRST rule in briefing + dashboard toggle

description: Per design decision (required should fail, not warn): blockOnMissingRequired() in tasks.ts now REFUSES the action and sets process.exitCode=1 when a required custom field is unset — at tasks create (task not created), tasks complete, and tasks status completed|in_review (transition refused). The error names each unset field + the exact fix command (--field … for create, tasks field … for transitions) + the field's prompt. Escape hatch for intentional drafts / automation: --allow-missing-required flag on create, or DREAMCONTEXT_ALLOW_MISSING_REQUIRED=1 env (honored by complete/status too), which downgrades the failure to a warning. Blast radius is the CLI command only — the dashboard create path (server route → backend.create) is unaffected, so quick-create still works. Verified: create-without-required → exit 1 + no file; --field or the escape → exit 0; complete with required empty → refused. Supersedes today's warn-only backstop entry. Skill ref + cli-reference + mirror updated.
summary: Required custom fields now HARD-FAIL the action (non-zero exit) at create/complete/advance instead of warning; --allow-missing-required escape for drafts

description: Root-caused a report that an agent created a task without setting a required custom field. Diagnosis: the data + snapshot were correct (override marks the field required; snapshot briefs [REQUIRED] + flags ⚠ UNSET), but the briefing is ADVISORY and a stale agent session never reloads it. Fix: warnUnsetRequiredFields() in src/cli/commands/tasks.ts — after tasks create, tasks complete, and tasks status completed|in_review, the CLI prints a loud warning naming each unset required custom field plus the exact 'dreamcontext tasks field <slug> <key> <value>' command (with the field's prompt as a hint). Because the global CLI is shared, this fires even for agent sessions whose in-context briefing predates the rebuild — the miss is surfaced in tool output at create/complete time, not silently. Smoke-verified: warns on create-without-field and on complete-with-required-unset; silent when the field is set. Skill ref updated.
summary: tasks create/complete/status now WARN loudly (with the exact fix command) when a required custom field is left unset — deterministic backstop independent of snapshot freshness

description: Two-part extension of the per-project custom-field system. (1) REQUIRED flag: CustomFieldDef gains required:boolean (overrides.ts parse + upsert + server POST /task-overrides/fields). renderOverrideBriefing now tags each field [REQUIRED]/[optional] and emits a hard rule that required fields must be set before a task leaves todo. (2) VISIBILITY: a task's custom-field VALUES are now surfaced to the agent without opening the file — the SessionStart snapshot Active Tasks block renders 'Custom fields: key=value, key=⚠ UNSET (required)' per task (snapshot.ts getActiveTaskEntries), and tasks list --long renders them too (TaskRecord.custom_fields added in task-query.ts). Skills updated behaviorally: SKILL.md Tasks-essentials + references/tasks-and-features.md document required + the inline-visibility + the mandatory-required rule; .claude/skills mirror re-synced. (3) DASHBOARD: TaskOverrideEditor now manages fields structurally — AddCustomFieldForm wired in with add AND edit (upsert by id) + a Required toggle; the raw markdown is now READ-ONLY (a generated preview; Save/Start-from-template removed) so template editing is not exposed in the UI; field cards show required/optional + edit/remove. Tests: overrides.test.ts asserts required parse + [REQUIRED] briefing; 88 affected unit tests green; end-to-end smoke confirmed snapshot + tasks list + doctor surface the fields and flag required-unset.
summary: Custom fields gain a required/optional flag and become visible to the agent everywhere (snapshot, tasks list, briefing); dashboard manages them structurally

description: The dashboard Version filter is now sprint-aware. state/.active-version.json holds the active planning version (the current sprint), re-validated against RELEASES.json on read so a released milestone can never silently capture new tasks. New routes GET/PUT /api/releases/active (registered before /api/releases/:version so the literal active is not captured by the matcher); PUT lazily creates a planning entry for a bare version string and returns 409 for an already-released version. VersionFilter.tsx distinguishes current/planning/released sprints with status icons, per-row Set-current and Mark-complete actions, search, and completed-at-bottom sort. Hooks: useActiveVersion/useSetActiveVersion/useCompleteVersion. Test: releases-active-route.test.ts.

description: Added src/lib/overrides.ts: a project drops _dream_context/overrides/task.md to shadow the default task shape. Frontmatter custom_fields declares a schema (name, key, type=text|number|select|date, options, sync=[clickup,github], prompt); body is the scaffold template plus an optional ## Agent Instructions section. Discovery is by file-presence so absent the file every consumer is byte-identical to defaults (zero regression). Values are set with tasks field <slug> <key> [value|clear] and tasks create --field key=value, validated against the schema, stored in task frontmatter custom_fields. Sync: ClickUp native fields (select=drop_down); GitHub select=<key>:<value> label, others=<!-- dc:fields --> body block; tasks provision reuses remote fields/labels by name. renderOverrideBriefing injects the field list + prompts + Agent Instructions into the SessionStart snapshot and every sub-agent. doctor validates the override (non-fatal warnings). Tests: overrides.test.ts, clickup-fields.test.ts, github-map.test.ts.
summary: Task-format & custom-field overrides via overrides/task.md (declared fields sync to ClickUp/GitHub; briefed to agents)
