Fast-forwarded main and pushed, which auto-closed all three PRs. Post-merge multi-reviewer PASS: backend and frontend findings all cleared (dangerouslySetInnerHTML pre-existing, KanbanBoard date filter uses non-nullable fields, calendar-utils uses correct local-time math).
summary: Merge PRs #67, #68, #69 via local integration: stash WIP → ordered merge → resolve conflict → tests green → fast-forward main

description: Add member-match.ts: a fuzzy-match layer that canonicalizes a person slug or display name against the live member roster returned by each backend (tasks members). Exact match wins; case-insensitive and slug-normalized fuzzy match follows; an ambiguous match aborts with an explicit error listing the candidates; an unrecognized name is recorded but emits a warning that the assignment won't sync until the person is a member. The silent fallback to the API token owner's account (a ClickUp behavior) is eliminated. tasks members CLI command exposes the roster for inspection. TaskDetailPanel shows an assignee chip row with the resolved names. Integration: merged via local conflict resolution (both PR #67 and #69 added helpers at the same location in tasks.ts; both kept, separated by a closing brace). 62 member-match unit tests + tasks-members-route tests added.
summary: Resolve assignees to real members via fuzzy match; warn on unmatched, never silently drop (PR #69)

description: Add a planned start date field to tasks, making the existing due date the end of a range. Both ends are independently settable and clearable via CLI (tasks start/tasks due with 'clear') and via the dashboard date picker with a clear button. An inverted range is rejected server-side. Setting either date on a backlog-tagged task removes the backlog tag automatically. Start and due dates sync to both the ClickUp and GitHub backends: ClickUp uses the native start/due fields; GitHub uses a 'dc:start:YYYY-MM-DD' label. github-map.ts was added to house the GitHub date serialization. Conformance tests added (clickup-start-date.test.ts, github-dates.test.ts, 170 cases). The dashboard Timeline/Gantt and Calendar views consume start_date for bar width and positioning, forward-compatible via a typed cast in calendar-utils.taskSpan().
summary: Clearable due dates + start/end date ranges reliably synced to ClickUp and GitHub backends (PR #67)

description: Added three time-axis task views to the dashboard, switchable from the existing view toggle and driven by the same filter set as Kanban/Eisenhower/RICE. Timeline/Gantt: status-colored bars created→due (prefers start_date when present) with today marker, overdue outline, adaptive ticks, and an Unscheduled tray. Calendar: Monday-first month grid with due-date chips, overdue/today highlighting, and due/unscheduled counts. Activity heatmap: GitHub-style 53-week grid with Created/Updated/Completed/Due metric toggle and an SR-only daily summary. Shared local-time date logic in calendar-utils.ts (taskSpan() is forward-compatible with the in-flight start_date feature via a typed cast). Verified via tsc -b, vite build, and live Playwright; multi-reviewer (design+engineering) iterated to PASS with 5 fixes (sparse-data heatmap scaling, after-midnight goToday, calendar-valid dateOf, SR-only heatmap list, aria-pressed toggles).
summary: Dashboard: timeline (Gantt), calendar & activity-heatmap task views

description: Before syncing the dreamcontext task list to GitHub Issues (taskBackend=github, repo meanllbrl/dreamcontext), the 85 completed task files that had never been pushed to GitHub were consolidated into 9 per-release 'shipped' issue-task files (shipped-v0-1-0-foundational through shipped-v0-9-0), one per release line. The originals were moved to _dream_context/state/archive/ via plain filesystem move. Safety confirmed: only dreamcontext tasks delete enqueues a remote close (soft-delete as not_planned); a plain archive move is sync-invisible because the non-recursive *.md glob used by tasks list and sync excludes the archive/ subdirectory. The 20 tasks already synced as closed GitHub issues #4-#36 were left untouched. This pre-sync hygiene keeps the GitHub tracker to one closed issue per release instead of ~100 fine-grained historical tasks.
summary: Consolidate 85 un-synced completed tasks into 9 per-release shipped issue-tasks; archive originals to state/archive/ pre-GitHub-sync

description: Add the 'curator' skill: an orchestrator (skill-curator/SKILL.md) that drives curator-audit, curator-executor (fan-out workers per domain: knowledge, features, tasks, versions), and curator-verifier sub-agents. Unlike sleep, which is conservative and additive, the curator is the periodic brain-refactor pass that is permitted to MOVE, MERGE, SPLIT, RENAME, RE-TYPE, and RETIRE. Scope per sub-agent: knowledge (summarize/compress bloated files, normalize tags to taxonomy, knowledge move into current folder hierarchy, merge duplicate/near-duplicate files and repoint wikilinks); single-source-of-truth enforcement (no topic as both a feature and knowledge file, no duplicate knowledge); features (reconcile status, rename to current vocabulary, dedup vs knowledge, flag stale); task backlog management (remove/merge stale or duplicate tasks, detect demonstrably-finished tasks, attach orphans to the right planning version); versions/releases (reconcile statuses). Installed as a CORE skill + CORE agents (always installed, same wiring as the initializer). Shipped the 'knowledge merge' primitive as a new CLI command to support curator's MERGE action. Dogfood run reorganized THIS brain: deduped overlapping knowledge files, reconciled task and feature statuses, normalized taxonomy, verified zero duplicate-topic docs and ZERO feature-vs-knowledge collisions after the run. Merged via PR #40, merge commit 71d875f.
summary: Sub-agent-driven brain-refactor skill (curator) + 'knowledge merge' primitive + dogfood reorg of this brain (dedup, status reconciliation, taxonomy normalization) (PR #40)

description: Replace the single dreamcontext-initializer agent with a full orchestrator skill (skill-initializer/SKILL.md) that drives three sub-agents: initializer-scout (codebase + material audit), initializer-ingestor (fan-out progressive ingestion into knowledge/feature/task hierarchy), and initializer-verifier (gated correctness check). The skill handles codebase-only repos via a lighter scout+ingest pass. Installed as a CORE skill via installCoreForPlatform (findPackageFile('skill-initializer',...), manifest kind 'core'), always present after setup, not an optional pack. The standalone agents/dreamcontext-initializer.md was deleted; the single-source-of-truth principle made two parallel bootstrap paths a violation. The SessionStart and UserPromptSubmit hooks were extended to auto-detect four new initializer trigger conditions beyond a missing _dream_context/: sparse brain (empty knowledge/, zero features, untouched stubs), migrate-from-folder, and mass-new-source; each condition fires an offer without a false-positive on a healthy brain. Merged via PR #39, merge commit fa4421f.
summary: Interactive sub-agent-driven brain bootstrap skill (scout->confirm->ingestor->verifier) + hook auto-detection of sparse/migrate/mass-source conditions; standalone initializer agent removed (PR #39)

description: Add GitHub Issues as a second remote task backend, reusing the issue-#11 pluggable TaskBackend interface and generic sync engine (api-adapter.ts/merge.ts/sync-state.ts). The data model treats the issue body as the task markdown document; only a 'completed' status closes the issue (state_reason: completed); 'delete' is a soft-delete via closed/state_reason: not_planned because GitHub REST cannot hard-delete. Open sub-statuses (todo/in_progress/in_review) ride on dc:* reserved-prefix labels; priority, urgency, tags, and version follow as separate label prefixes. Projects-v2 GraphQL custom fields are deferred to Tier-2. The implementation (22 files, +3376/-88) added src/lib/task-backend/github.ts (github-map + api-adapter), extended config/secrets shapes additively, wired CLI commands (dreamcontext config task-backend github), and matched the dashboard sync-status route. reconcileRemoteDeletions is a near-direct port of the ClickUp equivalent (same throttle, same watermark optimization, same pendingDeletes guard). 129 tests green. Merged via PR #38, merge commit 7b481d1.
summary: GitHub Issues as 2nd remote task backend: issue-body model, dc:* labels for sub-status, completed-closes/not_planned-soft-deletes (PR #38, 129 tests green)

description: Deleted the single-pass dreamcontext-initializer bootstrap agent (source + all platform mirrors). Its role is now owned by the new 'initializer' skill, which drives initializer-scout/ingestor/verifier and handles codebase-only repos via a light scout+ingest pass — a single bootstrap surface, no parallel agent. Rewired references in skill/SKILL.md, skill-initializer/SKILL.md, src/templates/AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md, root AGENTS.md, README.md, _dream_context/core/6.system_flow.md, src/cli/commands/init.ts comment, and the inbox follow-up note. Feature PRDs (project-initialization, tag-taxonomy) left for sleep to reconcile.
summary: Remove standalone dreamcontext-initializer agent — folded into the interactive 'initializer' skill

description: `dreamcontext knowledge move <slug> <folder>` — atomically moves a knowledge file into a context folder and rewrites every wikilink to it across the vault in the same operation. Merged to main via PR #37 (commit 2ee639f); issue #33 auto-closed. Landed on top of the v0.8.8 release commit, so it ships in the next npm cut (v0.9.0 planning bucket). Resolved a SKILL.md docs-restructure conflict on merge by porting the docs into references/*.md.
summary: knowledge move <slug> <folder>: atomic move + wikilink rewrite (PR #37, closes #33)

description: v0.8.8 shipped on npm (dreamcontext@0.8.8, verified live via npm view). Bundles four orthogonal concerns: (1) `dreamcontext upgrade` CASCADE — upgrades the CLI, then updates the macOS desktop app if installed, then offers to refresh every registered vault; `--yes` runs it unattended; runUpgrade is now async with injectable installer/vaultLister/projectUpdater/appInstalledCheck/appUpdater/confirmAll. (2) `dreamcontext setup` now offers the macOS desktop-app install (--install-app / --skip-app / DREAMCONTEXT_INSTALL_NO_APP=1), gated to never download unattended in non-TTY/CI. (3) The `dreamcontext` skill converted from a single 53KB SKILL.md into a skill FOLDER — lean always-loaded core (Capabilities-at-a-Glance map) + references/{cli-reference,integrations,tasks-and-features,knowledge-and-recall,sleep,improving-dreamcontext}.md, copied next to SKILL.md for both platforms by install-skill and recorded in the manifest as kind 'core'. (4) Agent + skill-pack compliance pass: removed retired dreamcontext-rem-sleep orchestrator refs (main agent runs the sleep flow), reframed diagrams to context-grouped folders, added the feature-vs-knowledge single-source-of-truth boundary. Validated by a 5-reviewer parallel orchestration + holistic sign-off; full suite ~1986 green, build + doctor clean.
summary: v0.8.8 npm: upgrade cascade + desktop-app setup offer + skill→folder + agent/pack compliance

description: Compliance sweep across every installed sub-agent and skill pack to align with conventions that shipped across v0.8.x but were only partially propagated. Removed retired dreamcontext-rem-sleep orchestrator references from sleep-migration, sleep-product, sleep-state, sleep-tasks, and council/debate-protocol (main agent runs the sleep flow — there is no rem-sleep dispatcher). Reframed Excalidraw SKILL.md diagram layout from a segregated knowledge/diagrams/ dump to context-grouped folders (board co-located in its context folder at knowledge/<context>/<title>/<title>.excalidraw.md); apply-diagrams is now documented as the legacy flat-board maintenance mechanism, not the new-board path. dreamcontext-explore updated to use knowledge/data-structures/ (not the stale core/data-structures/) and documents peer-read: the agent may read a connected peer's files directly or print its context via snapshot --vault. dreamcontext-initializer updated with the current skill-folder shape and the single-source-of-truth feature-vs-knowledge boundary. sleep-product gained explicit Rule 6 (single source of truth — feature vs knowledge). Council SKILL.md and debate-protocol.md changed rem-sleep references to sleep-flow/main-agent. Full test suite: 1986 green. Two isolated agent probes confirmed the updated definitions answer layout, data-structures-path, peer-read, and dispatch-authority questions correctly. Work is in the working tree, uncommitted.
summary: Bring all sub-agents and skill packs into compliance with current dreamcontext conventions (rem-sleep refs, diagram layout, data-structures path, peer-read, single-source-of-truth rule)

description: Investigated integrating headroom-ai (a Python context-compression proxy) to address context window overflow in long Claude Code sessions (issue #36). A goal-skill cycle built a Phase-1 implementation plan and two multi-review passes surfaced blocking issues: the eval A/B harness was scoped to tests/ but the eval script lived in eval/, the snapshot header strings used for splitting mismatched the actual emitted headers, and the proxy architecture introduced a dependency on a Python venv for a CLI tool that is fully Node/ESM. Decision: headroom-ai is the wrong layer — context compression is a Claude Code / API concern, not a dreamcontext concern. The implementation branch (feat/36-context-compression, worktree dreamcontext-issue-36) and all probe artefacts were cleaned up entirely. Issue #36 remains closed with this rationale as the closing comment. Working tree restored to clean main at 23c2b21.
summary: Explored and rejected headroom-ai context compression integration (issue #36 closed, wrong layer)

description: Pre-existing bug in src/lib/wikilink-rewrite.ts: splitOnCodeFences segmented the file into text/fence alternating parts and joined segments with join(''), losing the newline at every code fence boundary. This produced merged lines across the boundary (text line + fence delimiter concatenated without a newline) and dropped blank lines in the text segment following a fence. The bug was latent because knowledge files only get rewritten when a wikilink actually matches; the knowledge move command triggered it on files with fenced code examples. Fix: join('\n') — the segments are a contiguous partition of the original lines so this is provably lossless.
summary: wikilink-rewrite splitOnCodeFences: join('') dropped newlines at code fence boundaries — fixed to join('\n')

description: Add dreamcontext knowledge move <slug> <folder> command that atomically renames a knowledge file to a new location and rewrites all wikilinks in the vault that reference the old slug. The move is idempotent for crash recovery: if the destination already exists the rename is skipped but link rewriting still runs and the stale slug is cleaned from knowledge_access. The knowledge_access migration key always gets deleted after the move+merge so no dangling access-tracking entries remain. Wikilink rewriting uses src/lib/wikilink-rewrite.ts splitOnCodeFences for correct link detection in files that contain fenced code examples. This shipped on branch feat/knowledge-move-command as PR #37 (open, closes #33). Also fixed in the same PR: a pre-existing newline-loss bug in wikilink-rewrite.ts splitOnCodeFences where join('') dropped the boundary newline at every code fence boundary — changed to join('\n').
summary: dreamcontext knowledge move <slug> <folder>: atomic rename + wikilink rewrite across the vault (PR #37)

description: Four focused rule updates in SKILL.md. Rule 2 (skill triage) now explicitly states agents must only name skills that appear in their available-skills list — no phantom skill references. Rule 4 (single source of truth) adds a clear feature-vs-knowledge boundary: a topic is EITHER a feature PRD OR knowledge, never both; in-progress work lives in a task. Rule 5 (tasks) refines the five-minute threshold to 'extend don't fork' — check for an existing task and broaden it before creating a new one. New Rule 13 instructs agents that connected peer projects are readable via memory recall (which already spans peers) and direct file reads or dreamcontext-explore scoped to the peer path. Also: ClickUp reference reframed as a cloud/remote task backend; 'reading a specific peer' how-to added. Validated by spawning isolated agents with the new skill and verifying they correctly identified ClickUp sync, federation, and peer access as available.
summary: SKILL.md operational rules updated: skill-triage, single-source-of-truth, task-extension, and peer-reading clarified

description: Added a desktop-app install step at the end of dreamcontext setup on macOS. If the app is not already installed, setup now offers to run dreamcontext app install in the same session so a new machine comes up complete in one command. Three escape hatches: --install-app forces the install non-interactively, --skip-app suppresses it, and the environment variable DREAMCONTEXT_INSTALL_NO_APP=1 prevents any download (CI/scripts). In an interactive TTY without any flag the prompt defaults to yes. The step is silently skipped on non-macOS platforms, when the app is already installed, or in a non-interactive run without --install-app. The install is best-effort: a failure only prints a warning and the rest of setup continues normally.
summary: dreamcontext setup offers to install the macOS desktop app (--install-app / --skip-app / DREAMCONTEXT_INSTALL_NO_APP=1)

description: Expanded dreamcontext upgrade into a machine-wide updater cascade. After installing the new CLI version from npm, the command detects whether the macOS desktop app is installed and updates it via npx dreamcontext app update, then offers to refresh every registered project in the vault registry via npx dreamcontext update --yes in each project directory. With --yes the entire cascade runs non-interactively; in an interactive TTY the project refresh is confirmed with a single prompt; in a non-interactive context without --yes a hint is printed but nothing spawns unattended. One bad project never aborts the rest — per-vault results are reported individually. Five unit tests added for the new injection points. This makes dreamcontext upgrade the single command that keeps the whole machine current; agents must not run per-project updates by hand.
summary: dreamcontext upgrade cascades: CLI, then desktop app (if installed), then all registered projects

description: Rewrote the core dreamcontext skill from a single monolithic SKILL.md into a progressive-disclosure folder: a lean always-loaded SKILL.md (with a full Capabilities-at-a-Glance table naming every feature including ClickUp sync, dashboard, desktop app, federation, council, and marketing) plus six on-demand reference files (cli-reference.md, tasks-and-features.md, knowledge-and-recall.md, sleep.md, integrations.md, improving-dreamcontext.md). The SKILL.md front-loads what dreamcontext CAN do so agents stop misreporting missing capabilities; depth moves to references read only when the task demands it. installCoreForPlatform now copies the references/ directory and records each file in the install manifest so dreamcontext update and asset-drift checks keep them in sync. Motivation: recurring agent error where capabilities like ClickUp, federation, and the dashboard were denied as nonexistent because the old single-file skill named too little.
summary: dreamcontext skill is now a folder (SKILL.md + references/*.md) with a Capabilities-at-a-Glance map

description: Release v0.8.7 published on both channels. npm: dreamcontext@0.8.7 live on the registry (verified via npm view dreamcontext version). GitHub Release: non-draft, marked Latest at https://github.com/meanllbrl/dreamcontext/releases/tag/v0.8.7, with assets dreamcontext-beta_0.8.7_aarch64.app.tar.gz (27 MB) and matching .sha256, built by desktop-release.yml (build, ad-hoc sign, package, verify, publish all green) triggered by the v0.8.7 tag push. The release bundles all PR #35 work: role-based companion-knowledge indexing, Excalidraw canvas-editor board rendering, embedded-image asset endpoint (sharp/WebP, containment-guarded, 256-entry LRU), SQL-fence concatenation, live refresh, and the 4 post-merge review fixes. Also fixed in commit df47cfe: desktop/package.json was stuck at 0.6.0 through the entire 0.8.x line; realigned to 0.8.7 together with Cargo.toml and tauri.conf.json.
summary: v0.8.7 shipped on npm + GitHub (desktop .app); desktop/package.json realigned from 0.6.0 to 0.8.7

description: Four correctness and hygiene fixes applied in commit d30bac1 after PR #35 merged. (a) ExcalidrawPreview now gates the loading spinner on assetsQuery.isLoading rather than assetFiles === undefined; on an asset-fetch error the query settles (isLoading=false) so the board mounts and renders without images rather than spinning forever behind a permanent 'Loading diagram…' overlay. (b) Removed the dead low-quality tier and the quality query param from useKnowledgeAssets and the /api/knowledge-assets route — the dashboard only ever requested high quality and the canvas freezes initialData at mount, making a low→high progressive swap impossible; the misleading comments were corrected. (c) MAX_ASSETS_BYTES truncation now logs a warning that names the slug and the number of images dropped, and continues packing smaller images rather than hard-breaking on the first oversized one. (d) The in-memory assetCache is bounded to a 256-entry LRU (recency-bumping cacheGet + evicting cacheSet) to prevent unbounded memory growth in a long-lived dashboard server.
summary: Post-merge review fixes: spinner-on-error, dead two-pass removal, MAX_ASSETS_BYTES warning, 256-entry assetCache LRU

description: Add automatic live refresh to the dashboard so sleep debt, task lists, and knowledge entries update without the user switching pages or reloading. Global react-query refetchInterval set to 15 seconds, active only when the browser tab is visible (refetchIntervalInBackground: false) to avoid background traffic. A Refresh button in the Header provides on-demand re-fetch for users who want immediate sync after a sleep consolidation. The currently open knowledge document (identified by the active slug in KnowledgePage) is excluded from the polling query so a board in the canvas editor never reloads mid-pan and loses scroll state.
summary: Live refresh: 15s refetchInterval (visible-tab only) + header Refresh button; open board excluded from poll to prevent mid-pan reload

description: extractSchemaSql previously stopped at the first sql fenced block; if that block contained only comments (a common pattern for describing tables before listing column types), it produced no parsed entities and displayed 'No schema entities found'. Fix: concatenate all sql fenced blocks before parsing. SqlPreview also gains a fallback to display highlighted raw SQL when the parser finds no entities, so data-structure files never show a blank panel even when the SQL does not match the expected entity syntax.
summary: SQL preview: concatenate ALL sql fences so a comment-only first fence no longer yields 'No schema entities found'; fallback to raw SQL

description: Obsidian stores embedded screenshots in Excalidraw boards as ## Embedded Files wikilinks (not base64 inline data), so the dashboard canvas rendered blank placeholders where images should appear. New GET /api/knowledge-assets/:slug route (src/server/routes/knowledge.ts) parses the ## Embedded Files map from the board body, resolves each referenced path under the vault root (containment-guarded to prevent path traversal, images-only filter), downscales and recompresses to WebP via sharp (optional dependency — falls back to returning the original if sharp is unavailable), and caches the result keyed by path+mtime. The canvas component requests each asset at high-pass quality. The route accepts the board slug; the resolver picks the board's frontmatter-declared vault root for multi-vault safety.
summary: Board embedded-image resolution via GET /api/knowledge-assets/:slug — Obsidian wikilink paths resolved, downscaled to WebP via sharp

description: Replace the static exportToSvg() rendering path in ExcalidrawPreview with a live Excalidraw canvas editor in view mode (ExcalidrawCanvas.tsx). The motivation: a large board's exported SVG is rasterized once by WebKit/WKWebView and then sampled down as the user zooms, producing soft, blurry output. The canvas editor re-draws at every zoom level via its own renderer, so text and embedded screenshots stay crisp at any scale. New features: native wheel-pan / pinch-zoom, fit-and-center on first open (scrollToContent called after mount + layout-settle timer + ResizeObserver to guarantee it fires after flex-pane final size is known), loading spinner during bundle lazy-load. The ExcalidrawPreview component now acts as a dispatcher: .excalidraw slugs route to ExcalidrawCanvas; all other knowledge files continue to the existing markdown/mermaid path. Inline preview fills the detail pane like full-screen.
summary: Excalidraw boards render via real canvas editor (view mode) instead of exported SVG — crisp at all zoom levels, native pan/zoom

description: Replace the flat diagram list in the dashboard KnowledgePage with a recursive nested folder tree. Categories like diagrams/competitors/acme/ now nest as collapsible sub-folders instead of all collapsing into a single flat Diagrams list. A board's redundant self-named wrapper folder (<title>/<title>.excalidraw) is automatically collapsed for cleaner display. Excalidraw cards display a sketch icon so a diagram is recognizable at a glance. The 12 project boards were reorganized under system/ (architecture, federation, how-it-works, recall, system-overview), memory-science/ (memory-upgrade-plan, memory-uplift-comparison, neuroscience, sleep), and product/ (council, licensing, problem) subfolders. The data layer already used full nested slugs, so the backend needed no changes; build-all.mjs was updated to resolve boards by glob rather than fixed paths so it is robust to any nesting depth. SKILL.md and skill-packs/excalidraw/SKILL.md updated to document the optional category-subfolder convention; flat-regression and e2e tests updated to match foldered slugs.
summary: Nested diagram tree: dashboard renders diagrams/ as recursive collapsible folder tree; 12 boards reorganized under system/, memory-science/, product/

description: Add role-based companion-knowledge indexing so .md files that live beside an Excalidraw board in a per-title folder are no longer unconditionally excluded from the index, recall corpus, snapshot, and dashboard. The dark-sibling gate is now intent-driven: isDarkDiagramSibling() gains an optional isIndexableKnowledge arg (defaults false — backward-safe); a companion .md that carries name: frontmatter is treated as first-class knowledge and passed the flag by knowledge-index.ts and recall.ts (frontmatter is parsed once, no extra fs reads). Generator scripts (.board.cjs), spec JSON, and frontmatter-less helper notes remain dark. This allows co-located teardown and context docs (e.g. acme/acme.excalidraw.md + acme.teardown.md) to recall normally without moving files out of their natural board folder. Verified end-to-end via knowledge index --plain and memory recall.
summary: Role-based companion-knowledge indexing: co-located .md with name: frontmatter now indexes as first-class knowledge

description: Desktop app version in tauri.conf.json was still at 0.8.2 after the npm v0.8.5 publish. Bumped tauri.conf.json to 0.8.5 (commit 9d89b53), pushed the v0.8.5 tag, which triggered desktop-release.yml and produced the GitHub Release with the aarch64.app.tar.gz and .sha256 assets. npm v0.8.5 had been published separately; this commit aligned the desktop channel to match. v0.8.4 was npm-only (no desktop tag).
summary: v0.8.5 desktop GitHub Release: Tauri version realigned 0.8.2->0.8.5, v0.8.5 tag pushed, CI built aarch64 .app

description: Added Pass B0 to sleep-product so structural hygiene runs every consolidation cycle rather than only at one-time version-gated migrations. Step 1: calls dreamcontext migrations apply-diagrams each cycle to fold canonical flat boards into per-title subfolders and atomically rewrite wikilinks (idempotent; new boards that appear mid-flight are caught without a separate migration). Scratch boards in inbox/workspace are left in place. Step 2 (deep-only): groups knowledge files with 3+ files sharing a clear topic into knowledge/<group>/ subfolders; knowledge/**/*.md is indexed recursively so subfolder recall is unaffected. Mirror agent files (.claude/agents/, .codex/agents/prompts/) were also updated to match the canonical sleep-product.md after stale sections were found.
summary: sleep-product Pass B0 (Organize): ongoing idempotent diagram foldering + knowledge subfolder grouping every cycle

description: A test that referenced the flat knowledge/diagrams/recall.excalidraw.md board slug broke after the sleep-product folder-organization pass moved the board into a per-title subfolder (knowledge/diagrams/recall/recall.excalidraw.md). The test was updated to reference the new foldered slug so the suite stays green after diagram organization.
summary: Excalidraw flat-board regression: repoint test to foldered diagram slug after sleep-product org pass

description: Disabled HTML5 drag-and-drop in the Tauri webview via drag_drop_enabled(false) in lib.rs to prevent the webview intercepting drag events before the dashboard's own drag handlers could fire. Realigned all three Tauri version strings (tauri.conf.json, desktop/package.json, Cargo.toml) to 0.8.6; the Cargo.toml crate version had drifted at 0.8.2 since the v0.8.5 Tauri bump only updated tauri.conf.json. Both fixes were included in the squash-merge of PR #34.
summary: Desktop Tauri v0.8.6: webview HTML5 DnD fix + version realignment across tauri.conf.json/package.json/Cargo.toml

description: Singleton version tags (version:<v>) must be reconciled against the live remote tags returned in the PUT response, not the base snapshot. The base snapshot drifts whenever the tag value is changed via a bound custom FIELD rather than the tag itself (clickup.ts:1078). Fix strips all live version:* tags except the desired one and adds the desired tag if absent from the live set. Non-singleton tags continue to diff against the base snapshot so remote-only tags survive until a pull merge. Also in this cluster: ClickUp rate limiter dialed to ratePerMinute:90 (headroom under 100/min hard cap), maxRetries:5 with Retry-After exponential backoff, SyncReport.failedPushes[] structural field so partial pushes cannot masquerade as success, and sleep done auto-retries once on non-empty failedPushes before surfacing a red error with the stale task list.
summary: ClickUp version-tag drift fix: reconcile against live PUT-response tags, not base snapshot

description: Task assignment moved from a scalar 'assignee' field to person:<slug> tags as the single source of truth; the legacy scalar field migrates on read and is then deleted. Effective assignees = union of person tags and legacy field, filtered to the configured roster. ClickUp sync sends CREATE with assignees:ids and UPDATE with an {add, rem} delta. Dashboard surfaces a person picker chip row (not a text field) so users assign without knowing internal slugs. Removing an assignee via any UI path clears both the person tag and the legacy assignee field atomically. A new /api/tasks-members route returns the roster for the picker. Source: task:post-sleep-clickup-sync-rate-limit-headroom-retry-no-silent-fail
summary: Multi-assignee via person:<slug> tags as source of truth + ClickUp delta sync + dashboard chip picker

description: LauncherGraph now renders two distinct arc types: purple 'reads' wires (live cross-vault recall) and teal 'listens' wires (sleep digest sync). A new /api/federation/sync endpoint writes both sides of the consent handshake atomically (A:out + B:in) so the graph can wire sync paths without the user opening two Settings panels. Nodes show a plain-language relationship list ('Tilki listens to your changes', 'Tilki reads you live'). Toggle toolbar switches between Reads and Sync modes. Guide panel explains the two-layer model (live read vs sleep digest) with a mini diagram. NUL-byte in launcher.ts removed as part of this pass.
summary: Launcher federation network view: dual-wire graph (reads/sync), consent handshake endpoint, connection guide

description: Recall now auto-spans readable peers (direction out/both, non-stale, peer shareable) without any flag; default result count raised from 5 to 10. A new 'federation peers' command lists registered connections with live status. The SessionStart snapshot injects an ambient 'Connected projects' section from the local peer-summaries cache (state/.peer-summaries.json), keeping the hot-path lock-free. Introduced via the v0.8.5/0.8.6 federation overhaul; copy-sync (drain+digest-inbox) parked as a no-op — live-reference is the canonical read model.
summary: Federation read-awareness: recall spans readable peers by default, new 'federation peers' command, ambient Connected-projects snapshot

The release satisfies the client /releases/latest auto-update lookup so the in-app updater works end-to-end.

description: Enable webview HTML5 drag-and-drop in the Tauri desktop app by setting allowFileDrop: false in lib.rs, which was previously blocking native DnD events in the Tasks view. Version bumped from 0.8.5 to 0.8.6 across all three version sources (tauri.conf.json, package.json, Cargo.toml — which had drifted to 0.8.2 and was realigned). Built, signed, and installed as dreamcontext-beta_0.8.6_aarch64.dmg to ~/Applications and /Applications; both the version string and the DnD fix verified in the installed bundle. Note: npm CLI and desktop app version lines are independent (per existing technical decision); package.json at 0.8.6 is a desktop-patch bump that does not conflict with the v0.9.0 feature planning milestone.

description: Three hardening behaviors shipped together. First, the ClickUp adapter now initialises with ratePerMinute: 90 (deliberate headroom under ClickUp's 100/min hard cap) and maxRetries: 5 with Retry-After-respecting exponential backoff — previously ran at exactly 100 with zero margin, reliably hitting 429 at the window edge during full post-sleep bulk pushes. Second, SyncReport gains a structural failedPushes: string[] field; pushLocal records any un-pushable slug so a partial push can no longer masquerade as success. Third, sleep done auto-retries the full sync once when failedPushes is non-empty, then emits a red error() 'Task sync INCOMPLETE — these tasks are stale on remote' + dreamcontext tasks sync hint if any remain. Root cause of the prior hidden bug: sleep done printed the error dim as report.errors[0]. Tests: push 12/12, backend 31, sleep 74 all green.
summary: ClickUp post-sleep sync hardening: rate margin, SyncReport.failedPushes, and visible failure in sleep done

description: Add a merge-precedence guard to the tasks-members route so task-derived assignee entries (id='') only fill gaps when no remote member record exists for a slug; remote members overwrite last and a real id can never be replaced by a stub. Wrap taskAssigneeMembers' list()+get() chain in a single try/catch that returns [] on any error, preventing a bad task read from DoS-ing the members endpoint. Reviewer PASS, 23 tests green.
summary: Local-first assignee-candidate fix: task-derived entries fill gaps without clobbering real member ids

description: Fix stale eval hygiene: 21 of 60 lines in eval/gold.jsonl referenced task/* slugs that no longer exist — all tasks were consolidated into features and knowledge files over prior sleep cycles, leaving the tasks directory empty. Updated every dead ref to its live feature/knowledge successor, preserving each query's intent and the curated alt structure. Recall quality stayed healthy: recall@3 95%, recall@1 81.7% on the full 60-query gold set, confirming the repointed targets rank correctly. The eval/ directory (and _dream_context/) are not in the npm package so this is maintenance-only — no shipped product code changed. Verified the two other test failures observed (excalidraw-knowledge, recall-capture-stress) were pre-existing environment artifacts from a concurrent background consolidation rewriting the live vault mid-test, not caused by this change.
summary: Repoint 21 dead task/* refs in eval/gold.jsonl to live feature/knowledge successors; recall@3 95%, recall@1 81.7%

description: Fix a bug where a ClickUp task could end up with two version tags (version:x + version:y) after a version change. Root cause: the push-diff reconciled the version tag against the base snapshot, but when version is changed via a bound custom FIELD (clickup.ts:1078 — the field outranks the tag), the merge writes the field value to base.version without updating the remote version tag. On the next push, the diff removes a phantom version tag (the one in the base snapshot) and leaves the real stale one. Fix in src/lib/task-backend/clickup.ts: non-version tags continue to diff against the base snapshot (so remote-only tags survive); version:* tags are now reconciled against the live remote tags returned by the PUT response — every live version:* tag other than the desired one is removed, and the desired tag is added if absent. This guarantees exactly one version tag survives after any version change regardless of how the version was last set. Regression test added to tests/unit/clickup-tags.test.ts covering the drift scenario (base snapshot unaware of stale remote version tag). 43 clickup tests pass, typecheck clean.
summary: Fix version-tag drift: reconcile version:<v> against live PUT response tags, not base snapshot

description: Replace the scalar assignee field with a person-tag-based multi-assignee system. Person tags ('person:<slug>') are the single source of truth for assignment; the legacy scalar 'assignee' field migrates to a person tag on read and is deleted from frontmatter. The effective assignee list is derived by unioning person tags with the scalar field and filtering to roster members. ClickUp sync sends CREATE with 'assignees: [ids]' and UPDATE with an add/rem delta; unknown slugs are silently dropped. The server route (routes/tasks.ts) resolves the roster+remote member union by slug (remote-priority, provider-agnostic — no 'clickup' substring in the match). Dashboard: TaskDetailPanel now renders a multi-select assignee dropdown (person tags shown as chips with × removal) rather than a single text field; adding an assignee adds a person tag, removing one removes the tag and clears the legacy assignee field atomically to prevent ghost reappearance. Two ghost-assignment bugs fixed across three review rounds (FAIL→FAIL→PASS): the Assignees chip row correctly calls setAssignees (sets assignee:null), and handleTagsChange also clears assignee:null when the backing person tag is removed — guarded narrowly to avoid data loss on unrelated tag edits. 1975 tests pass, typecheck clean.
summary: Multi-assignee via person tags: ClickUp delta sync, dashboard dropdown picker, ghost-reappearance fix

description: Add cross-project read awareness to the snapshot and launcher network view. The SessionStart hook now surfaces connected peer vaults and their recall availability in the snapshot (federation-aware context). The launcher federation graph wires updated to reflect the read-only model: purple 'reads (live)' edges represent live recall connections, not digest sync; the consent handshake is enforced at the graph/connection layer. New tests cover federation consent and purge paths; federation-cycle tests deleted (sync/drain cycle no longer applies). Changes in tests/unit/federation-consent.test.ts and new tests/unit/federation-purge.test.ts; tests/unit/federation-cycle.test.ts removed. Launcher federation graph (LauncherGraph.tsx), useLauncher.ts, and snapshot.ts updated for read-only semantics.
summary: Cross-project read awareness: snapshot hook and launcher wires show live federation read edges + consent handshake

description: Replace the copy-based sync+drain model with a read-only live-reference architecture where each vault holds its own canonical knowledge and peers are queried live at recall time. The recall hook now live-reads connected peers (via crossVaultRecall) rather than ingesting copies; sync and drain are preserved as inert no-ops for future roadmap use but no longer do anything. New 'federation purge' CLI command removes all federated knowledge materialised from a specific peer, with symlink-escape protection and atomic .connections.json writes with a nonce. The lib copy-code is parked in-tree for the eventual redesign. README and DEEP-DIVE updated: federation intro rewritten to 'recall across each other live — nothing is ever copied'; CLI command refs updated to federation peers/status/purge; sync/drain marked as inert; copy-based sync noted as parked on the roadmap; 'Read-only by construction' section documents the real guards. The federation Excalidraw diagram alt-text updated (rendered image still shows old push/pull model; pending npm run diagrams regeneration). This was driven by a user correction that 'reads' connections were copying content instead of referencing — single-source-of-truth is a hard requirement.
summary: Pivot federation from copy-sync to read-only live-reference model; add federation purge command

description: Add two targeted suppressions to the snapshot/hook pipeline. (1) Asset-drift scoping: the SessionStart 'update your dreamcontext' nag now stays silent when a content check confidently proves that no installed asset for this project would change on update — i.e. the CLI bump only touched skill-packs this project does not use. New src/cli/commands/asset-drift.ts (computeUsedAssetsChanged: temp-installs the real installers for the project's exact platforms+packs, byte-compares against disk, excludes settings.json and _dream_context/) and src/lib/asset-drift-cache.ts (persists verdict at state/.asset-drift.json, keyed on cliVersion+setupVersion). snapshot.ts getDriftDirective() reads the cache: when state=stale or bootstrap AND the cache confidently reports clean, the nag is suppressed; anything uncertain (no cache, version-mismatched, real change, error) falls open to the nag. The cache is refreshed in a detached process (hook refresh-asset-drift, new command) spawned on the existing once/24h tick in hook.ts, so the async installer stdout never contaminates the sync snapshot path. (2) Dashboard auto-open suppression: hook ensure-dashboard exits early when readAppManifest() !== null (the desktop beta app is installed), preventing a redundant browser tab alongside the native window. 13 new unit tests; full suite 1947 passed, build+typecheck clean.
summary: Scope the SessionStart drift nag to actively-used skills; suppress dashboard auto-open when the beta app is installed

description: Remove the hard rule that sleep/goal agents always bump touched tasks to in_review, never completed. New policy across agents/sleep-tasks.md, skill/SKILL.md, and skill-packs/goal-skill/SKILL.md: mark completed when the work is demonstrably done, low-risk, and already validated (chores, docs, mechanical refactors, tests-green fixes); use in_review only when a human genuinely must verify something (user-facing behaviour change, design/architecture decision, risky or critical-path work, or to hand over a close decision on superseded/abandoned work). Goal-skill Phase 6 PASS now directly marks completed rather than stopping at in_review and asking the user. Also extends sleep-tasks Step 6 from a staleness sweep to full backlog grooming: pivot-relevance reconcile (obsoleted tasks re-versioned or surfaced for close), version re-attach, and tag normalization to taxonomy facets each cycle.
summary: Replace reflexive in_review bumps with reality-based status policy across sleep-tasks + goal-skill agents

Adds src/lib/sleep-consolidation.ts (consolidationDepth() / isDestructiveAllowed() API — debt-based depth inference: light/standard/deep with deep unlocking merge-and-delete), salience.ts refactor (multi-signal scoring), src/lib/attribution.ts (multi-person author attribution helper), eval/sleep-quality/ harness (scorer.ts, gold.json, BASELINE.md, RESULTS.md — scored 57→100), tests/unit/sleep-consolidation.test.ts + sleep-quality-eval.test.ts + updates to sleep-system-360.test.ts (429 lines). description: Overhaul the desktop app's Launcher (main window) across three dimensions. (1) Per-project status dots: GREEN = up to date, YELLOW = project's setupVersion is behind the running CLI (needsUpdate = compareVersions(setupVersion, dreamcontextVersion()) < 0) with an inline Update button that runs 'dreamcontext update' in that project directory, RED = folder gone and removable. New backend endpoints: GET /api/launcher/status, POST /api/launcher/update, POST /api/launcher/unregister (src/server/routes/launcher.ts). (2) Interactive cross-project federation graph (react-force-graph-2d on a dedicated LauncherGraph.tsx page): Excalidraw-style drag-to-connect 'reads' arrows (A→B = A reads B, stored as an 'out' connection on A's side per the federation out/both model), flowing particle 'electric current' on active links, two-way links, click-to-remove, and per-node shareable toggle. New endpoints: GET /api/launcher/federation-graph, POST /api/launcher/connection, POST /api/launcher/connection/remove, POST /api/launcher/shareable. (3) Brain graph settings persisted server-side per project at _dream_context/state/.brain-settings.json via new src/server/routes/ui-settings.ts route — useGraphSettings.ts hydrates from server + writes through — so settings survive the desktop app's per-launch loopback-port change (which resets localStorage). Also rewrites ConnectionsManager.tsx with a plain-language explainer, Sharing card, and self-describing direction controls (Read from / Share to / Two-way) with per-option hints and new i18n keys. +12 unit tests in tests/unit/launcher-federation.test.ts; full suite green (1934 passed).
summary: Add per-project update-status dots, interactive federation graph, and server-side brain-settings persistence to Launcher (v0.8.3)

description: Two load-bearing architectural decisions recorded for 2.memory.md (could not write directly due to session permissions). (1) Animated WebP over video: WKWebView blocks video autoplay unconditionally — the muted HTML attribute is ignored because WKWebView checks the muted DOM PROPERTY; even setting it via ref+play() on mount is insufficient. Fix: serve mascot clips as animated WebP via GET /api/sleepy/anim?mode= and render with img — img autoplays animated WebP unconditionally in WKWebView, no JS needed. Do NOT revert to video for mascot clips. (2) Desktop user-tool spawn requires -ilc: A non-interactive login shell (-lc) skips ~/.zshrc — tools added to PATH there (e.g. ~/.local/bin, where claude lives) are invisible. Use /bin/zsh -ilc so the full terminal PATH is available in a Finder-launched app. Capture stdout/stderr separately so rc-file init noise never pollutes tool output. Sources: commit:b30d311, commit:1a69f7a, file:src/server/routes/launcher.ts, task:sleepy-notch-capture
summary: DECISION: animated WebP over video for WKWebView mascot; -ilc for user-tool spawn in desktop app

description: Replace the fire-and-forget child 'dreamcontext memory remember' spawn in POST /api/launcher/capture with a direct in-process insertToJsonArray call — the server IS dreamcontext, so it can write the CHANGELOG entry itself without resolving a CLI from PATH. In a packaged desktop app the login-shell CLI can be a stale/older global or absent entirely, causing a guaranteed-capture failure. This removes that entire failure class. Separately, /api/vaults now returns an 'exists' boolean per entry; the CaptureBar vault picker filters out dead vaults so they cannot be selected; the bar surfaces the server's real error message instead of a bare 'failed'. Three stale vaults (Test, Test2, delete-later) were removed from the vault registry. The claude enrichment spawn was also hardened with an 'error' event listener so an unresolvable binary cannot crash the server.
summary: Capture reliability: write CHANGELOG entry in-process instead of spawning child CLI; surface real error; filter dead vaults via /api/vaults exists flag

Add Sleepy, a desktop-only macOS notch quick-capture companion. A global hotkey (tauri-plugin-global-shortcut, registered on the permanent launcher window so it survives vault-window close) toggles a transparent top-center notch window: a 200×92 black panel (macOSPrivateApi transparent window) showing the Dreamy mascot whose mood follows the selected project's sleep debt (idle ≤3 / sleepy 4–9 / sleeps ≥10), with an auto-growing Apple-style multi-line textarea below (Enter submits, Shift+Enter inserts a newline). Submit POST /api/launcher/capture triggers instant dreamcontext memory remember (guaranteed) then fire-and-forget headless claude -p (injection: don't ask follow-ups, take notes and learn; note passed as explicit arg, no shell injection). Three mascot video clips downscaled to ~160 KB each are bundled as Tauri resources and served via GET /api/sleepy/video?mode= with Range support for WKWebView. Config (enabled flag + hotkey) is persisted to ~/.dreamcontext/sleepy.json so it survives per-launch port changes that would otherwise reset localStorage origin. Settings page gains an 'Enable Sleepy' toggle (default off) plus hotkey capture UI, visible desktop-only. Desktop-only — nothing shipped to npm. Visual design still iterating (not yet user-accepted).

description: First tagged desktop release v0.8.1 was cut via the new CI workflow and verified end-to-end: dreamcontext app install successfully pulled the asset from /releases/latest, verified the .sha256 checksum, passed codesign --verify --deep, and installed to ~/Applications with no Gatekeeper quarantine (ditto transfer preserves the ad-hoc signature). The continuous-app-update loop is now proven end-to-end. Desktop version 0.8.1 was chosen (not 0.8.0) to ensure the bundle version > last installed manifest version so the update loop does not trigger an infinite re-download cycle. CLI npm 0.8.x and desktop v0.8.x version numbers are coincidental, not coupled.

The release bundles: default-on CLI auto-upgrade (maybeTriggerUpgrade on session start), dreamcontext app install|update command, the continuous desktop update loop (maybeTriggerAppUpdate), self-contained CLI bundle (noExternal), and the desktop thin-shell. Tauri icon regeneration was run to produce all required platform sizes (32×32, 64×64, 128×128, 128×128@2x, Windows square tiles, Android mipmap sets, StoreLogo). No code changes; purely asset update.

description: The in-app onboarding wizard now exposes platform and skill-pack selection in both the new-project and existing-folder paths. Backend: ScaffoldAnswers gains platforms[] + packs[]; scaffoldProject normalizes platforms (defaults to ['claude']) and passes the list to init/setup; validates packs against the catalog and runs install-skill --packs --platforms after setup. New GET /api/launcher/catalog (vault-agnostic) serves the platform + pack choices. Strict-pick on the scaffold body: arrays filtered to strings; packs validated against known catalog names so no arbitrary args reach the CLI shell. Existing-folder flow routes through platform + skill-pack steps (STEPS_EXISTING_HASCTX path) so users can add platforms (e.g. Codex) and install skill packs on connect. description: After installing the CLI, on macOS (uname Darwin) install.sh now runs 'dreamcontext app install' so a fresh curl|sh setup also places the desktop app in ~/Applications. Best-effort: skipped silently if no desktop release is published yet or the download fails — never aborts the CLI install. Non-macOS platforms skip entirely. Opt out with DREAMCONTEXT_INSTALL_NO_APP env var. The npm install -g path stays CLI-only by design; this app step is specific to the curl|sh installer.
summary: install.sh installs the desktop app on macOS (best-effort, opt-out via DREAMCONTEXT_INSTALL_NO_APP)

description: Add .github/workflows/desktop-release.yml. On a v* tag push (or manual dispatch) it builds the dreamcontext-beta Tauri .app on macos-14 (aarch64), ad-hoc DEEP-signs it with entitlements (no Apple Developer ID / notarization), packages it as dreamcontext-beta_<ver>_aarch64.app.tar.gz plus a mandatory .sha256, verifies (extract + checksum -c + codesign --verify --deep), and publishes both to a non-draft GitHub Release. This is the factory + shelf the already-shipped client code (app install|update + maybeTriggerAppUpdate) needs: with a live release it can pull the arch-matching asset from /releases/latest, verify the checksum, and install via ditto (no quarantine bit, no Gatekeeper notarization) with zero further client changes.
summary: Release workflow for Tauri .app: v* tag → macos-14 build → ad-hoc deep-sign + entitlements → .tar.gz + .sha256 → GitHub Release

description: Add src/lib/ensure-cli.ts with ensureCliInstalled(): best-effort, never-throws helper that probes command -v dreamcontext via the user's LOGIN shell ($SHELL -lc) so a Finder-launched Tauri app sees nvm/brew PATH entries that are absent from the default non-login env. When the CLI is missing it runs npm install -g dreamcontext@latest. The scaffold handler calls it post-scaffold and surfaces {cli:{status}} (present|installed|failed) in the response; the wizard success screen shows a relevant status message. Rationale: scaffolded projects install .claude/ hooks that call npx dreamcontext hook ... — if no global CLI exists npx would hang or fail silently in a non-interactive hook context. Four unit tests cover present/installed/npm-absent/install-failed paths. Full suite: 1784 green.
